title: 'Til Death Do Us Part
description: The Prophets have told Sisko they do not approve of his impending marriage. Having been captured by the Breen, Ezri and Worf still don't know why the Breen would even bother to capture them. Some Cardassians are beginning to think their alliance with the Dominion was a grave mistake. Gul Dukat, disguised as a Bajoran, sets foot on DS9, and continues to unfurl his plan.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bradley Thompson, David Weddle

description: The Enterprise transports an elderly Starfleet Admiral to negotiate a hostage crisis. When the Admiral takes a de-aging drug, he endangers both his life and the mission.
writer: Michael Michaelian, D.C. Fontana, Johnny Dawkins, Trivia, Gene Roddenberry, Maurice Hurley

description: Federation time ship Capt Braxton pulls Seven out of her time to help identify and destroy a bomb planted aboard Voyager.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bryan Fuller, Nick Sagan

title: A Fistful of Datas
description: While the crew is experiencing some much needed down time, Worf and his son go on an Old West excursion in the holodeck, where they meet several versions of Data serving as the bad guy after an experiment in Engineering goes wrong.
writer: Robert Hewitt Wolfe, Brannon Braga, René Echevarria

title: A Man Alone
description: Ibudan, a criminal Odo has dealt with before, returns to DS9 only to be murdered shortly after - leaving Odo to be the prime suspect. Jake and Nog's trouble-making prompt Keiko to do something helpful for the station's children.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Gerald Sanford

title: A Mathematically Perfect Redemption
description: A wayward Starfleet ensign struggles to find a path to redemption.

title: A Matter Of Honor
description: Under an Officers Exchange Program, Riker serves aboard a Klingon ship, whose aging captain seeks an unwarranted bloody retaliation for suspected Enterprise treachery.
writer: Burton Armus, Wanda M. Haight, Gregory Amos

title: A Matter Of Time
description: Reaching Penthara IV after an asteroid wreaks havoc of catastrophic proportions, the Enterprise crew deals with trying to save the planet as well as deal with someone who claims to be a historian from the future.
writer: Rick Berman, Brannon Braga, René Echevarria

title: A Matter of Perspective
description: The widow of respected scientist Dr. Nel Apgar accuses Riker of seducing her and killing her husband. Rather than turn Riker over for unfavorable trial, the Enterprise reconstructs the facts.
writer: Ed Zuckerman, Ronald D. Moore

title: A Moral Star, Part 1
description: The crew forgo their dreams of Starfleet to return to Tars Lamora in a no-win scenario.

title: A Moral Star, Part 2
description: When their plan goes awry, the crew must improvise. Meanwhile, Gwyn discovers a dark truth that will forever jeopardize their quest toward salvation.

title: A Night in Sickbay
description: After previously offending the Kreetassans, Enterprise attempts to make a better second impression, only to upset the alien race once again. A frustrated Captain Archer returns from the planet only to be further upset to find that his dog Porthos, has been affected by a pathogen native to the world. While Phlox works around the clock treating Porthos, Archer stands vigil in sickbay, experiencing first-hand the quirky everyday life of his Denobulan Doctor.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Rick Berman, André Bormanis

title: A Piece of the Action
description: The Enterprise is guided to a distant, long-dead world where survivors of an extremely ancient race - existing only as disembodied energy - desiring the bodies of Kirk, Spock and astro-biologist Ann Mulhall so that they may live again.
writer: John T. Dugan

title: A Private Little War
description: Kirk, Uhura and Chekov are trapped on a planet where abducted aliens are enslaved and trained to perform as gladiators for the amusement of bored, faceless aliens.
writer: Margaret Armen

title: A Quality of Mercy
description: In the season one finale, just as Captain Pike thinks he's figured out how to escape his fate, he's visited by his future self, who shows him the consequences of his actions.

title: A Simple Investigation
description: Odo becomes romantically attached to a woman working with the Orion Syndicate.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, David Weddle

title: A Taste of Armageddon
description: Kirk and Spock must save their ship's crew when they are all declared killed in action in a bizarre computer simulated war where the actual deaths must nevertheless occur.
writer: Robert Hamner, Gene L. Coon

title: A Time to Stand
description: Borrowing a captured Jem'Hadar attack ship, Sisko and crew embark on a mission to destroy the hidden base where all of the ketracel-white is stored for the entire Alpha Quadrant.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, David Weddle

title: Absolute Candor
description: On the way to Freecloud, Picard requests a stop at a Romulan refugee planet where he reunites with Elnor, the young Romulan he befriended fourteen years ago; Narek continues to learn more about Soji.

title: Accession
description: When Worf destroys a civilian shuttle during an engagement with the Klingons, an extradition hearing is held to see if he must face charges.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, Bradley Thompson

title: Acquisition
description: Almost the entire crew is left unconscious when four Ferengi pirates raid Enterprise. A still conscious Trip is the only one who can get rid of them.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Maria Jacquemetton, André Jacquemetton

title: Affliction
description: While Enterprise is visiting Earth, Phlox is abducted by the Klingons and forced to research a cure for a virus destined to wipe out the race. Reed is tapped by a secret agency and ordered to betray Archer. Trip transfers to the Columbia.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman

title: Afterimage
description: Sisko tries to convince Ezri to stay in Starfleet and serve aboard DS9, as Ezri tries to get to know Jadzia's friends aboard the station, especially Worf. Meanwhile, Garak tries to deal with a severe bout of claustrophobia.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, David Weddle

title: Albatross
description: Dr. McCoy is arrested and charged with causing an alien plague.

title: Alice
description: Voyager takes on a "haunted" hot rod alien spacecraft with a possessive artificial intelligence that jealously bonds with Tom Paris.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bryan Fuller, Michael Taylor

title: All Good Things...
description: Capt. Picard finds himself shifting continually into the past, future and present and must use that to discover a threat to humanity's existence.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, Brannon Braga, René Echevarria

title: All Our Yesterdays
description: When Kirk, Spock and McCoy investigate the disappearance of a doomed planet's population, they find themselves trapped in different periods of that world's past.
writer: Jean Lisette Aroeste, Arthur H. Singer

title: All Those Who Wander
description: Enterprise crew comes face-to-face with their demons - and scary monsters too - when their landing party's stranded on a barren planet with a ravenous enemy.

title: All the World's a Stage
description: The crew answers a distress call to find a colony trapped in Starfleet's past.

title: Allegiance
description: Picard is kidnapped and held with three different aliens and meanwhile replaced with a replica as impostor captain.
writer: Richard Manning, Hans Beimler, Ronald D. Moore

title: Alliances
description: Janeway tries to make an alliance with a Kazon sect for their own protection and so they can continue their journey through the Delta Quadrant.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Lisa Klink

title: Alter Ego
description: Ens. Kim and Lt. Tuvok become rivals in love over what at first seems to be a holodeck character.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Lisa Klink

title: Amok Time
description: Primitive inhabitants of Gamma Trianguli VI worship a god who orders them to kill visitors from the Enterprise.
writer: Max Ehrlich, Gene L. Coon

title: An Embarrassment Of Dooplers
description: Mariner and Boimler try to track down the location of a legendary Starfleet party while the bridge crew deals with an insecure alien diplomat.

title: And the Children Shall Lead
description: Lovely telepath Miranda is aide to Ambassador Kollos, in a box to stop insanity when humans see Medusans. She rejects Larry, a designer of Enterprise, and senses murderous intent nearby.
writer: Jean Lisette Aroeste, Arthur H. Singer

title: Angel One
description: The Enterprise docks at a starbase for repairs where it is commandeered by a race of technologically-linked aliens intent on using the vessel for their own purposes.
writer: Maurice Hurley, Robert Lewin

title: Anomaly
description: After an attack by Osaarian pirates, Enterprise follows them to a mysterious sphere.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman, André Bormanis

title: Apocalypse Rising
description: With the war between Klingons and Federation intensifying, Sisko must make a plan to expose Gowron as a changeling. Odo is still having trouble with becoming a solid.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Aquiel
description: Murderous intrigue abounds for the Enterprise when one of the crew aboard a subspace station is believed dead, and suspected to have taken part in it until the Klingons show up with the young lieutenant, to Geordi's taste.
writer: Brannon Braga, Ronald D. Moore, Jeri Taylor

title: Arena
description: The Enterprise is thrown back in time to 1960s Earth.
writer: D.C. Fontana

title: Armageddon Game
description: Chief O'Brien, who still doesn't particularly like Doctor Bashir, must rely on him when the two are stranded on a planet and O'Brien is infected with a deadly, fast moving disease.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Morgan Gendel, Robert Hewitt Wolfe, Trivia

title: Ashes to Ashes
description: Voyager receives a former crewman, deceased, resurrected, genetically altered, unrecognizable, and glad to be "home." Meanwhile, looking after the Borg children exasperates Seven of Nine.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Doherty, Ronald Wilkerson

title: Assignment: Earth
description: While back in time observing Earth in 1968, the Enterprise crew encounters the mysterious Gary Seven who has his own agenda on the planet.
writer: Art Wallace, Gene Roddenberry

title: Assimilation
description: Picard and the crew travel back to 2024 Los Angeles in search of the one person who can help them identify the point at which time diverged.

title: Asylum
description: At the edge of Federation space, the crew applies for asylum at a comm relay outpost, only for their starship to reveal its shocking true purpose.

title: Attached
description: Captain Picard and Dr. Crusher discover things about one another when they are captured by an opposing alien race, due to the fact that the Enterprise has come to allow the planet's other civilization the choice of being part of the Federation.
writer: Nick Sagan, René Echevarria, Naren Shankar

title: Author, Author
description: For the first time in seven years, Voyager gets a mission from Starfleet Command: retrieve the 21st century probe Friendship One from a nearby planet.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Taylor, Bryan Fuller

title: Awakening
description: T'Pol and Archer confront the Syrrannites and meet T'Pau who denies the embassy bombing. Archer, who has Surak's essence, is asked to help. V'Las threatens Enterprise to make sure an attack on the Syrrannites isn't witnessed.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, André Bormanis

title: Azati Prime
description: Archer decides he should pilot a kamikaze shuttle to destroy the Xindi weapon. Temporal agent Daniels urges him to reconsider.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Manny Coto, Rick Berman

title: Babel
description: The crew and civilian population of Deep Space Nine begin to speak gibberish when a plague - an 'aphasia virus' - engineered by the Bajorans as a weapon against the Cardassians, accidentally gets released into the station's atmosphere.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael McGreevey, Naren Shankar

title: Babel One
description: Enterprise escorts Tellarites to peace talks with Andorians. While transporting they rescue Andorians and say the Tellarites attacked them. Enterprise is soon attacked by an Andorian ship, and Archer must prove who is responsible.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman, André Bormanis

title: Badda-Bing, Badda-Bang
description: When a programming glitch won't go away, mobsters take over Vic's and open for business, but the DS9 gang takes it personal when their favorite hangout is no longer their favorite, and they plan to get the new management ousted.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, David Weddle

title: Balance of Terror
description: The Enterprise discovers a planet exactly like Earth, but the only inhabitants are children who contract a fatal disease upon entering puberty.
writer: Adrian Spies

title: Bar Association
description: A man claiming to be the emissary comes through the wormhole. Sisko is happy to give up his position. Meanwhile Keiko returns and O'Brien stops spending time with Bashir.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Jane Espenson, René Echevarria

title: Barge of the Dead
description: A brutal experience aboard a shuttle puts B'Elanna in touch with the Klingon afterlife, where the Barge of the Dead steers her towards Klingon hell along with her mother.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bryan Fuller, Ronald D. Moore

title: Basics, Part I
description: Seska knows Voyager, and her Kazon cohorts want it, so the Voyager crew wonders what to make of her distress call announcing the birth of Chakotay's son.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink

title: Basics, Part II
description: With almost the entire crew of Voyager marooned on a desolate planet by the Kazon, Tom Paris heads out to fetch help while Lon Suder and the Doctor, still on Voyager, try to assist.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink

title: Battle Lines
description: While showing Bajoran spiritual leader Kai Opaka the wormhole, she, Sisko, Bashir and Kira crash land on a moon. The locals are in a never-ending war where they cannot die.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Richard Danus, Evan Somers

title: Before and After
description: On her deathbed, Kes relives her life, jumping backwards in spurts of time towards birth, gaining knowledge as to why as she goes.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Lisa Klink

title: Behind the Lines
description: After forming an attack plan on the Dominion, Sisko relinquishes command of the Defiant to Dax after accepting a promotion. On DS9, the resistance faces discovery when Odo links with another changeling.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, David Weddle

title: Bem
description: The Enterprise crew is being observed to evaluate their suitability to meet with advanced civilizations.

title: Beyond the Farthest Star
description: The Enterprise finds an ancient abandoned starship with a malevolent entity aboard, eager to take over the Starfleet ship.

title: Birthright, Part I
description: While visiting Deep Space 9, a Yridian tells Worf his father lives, and a discharge from an alien device puts Data in a dream state where he meets his own father.
writer: Brannon Braga, René Echevarria, Naren Shankar

title: Birthright, Part II
description: In seeking his father, Worf discovers a prisoner-of-war camp that has evolved into a Klingon/Romulan haven - an Eden where Worf becomes their serpent.
writer: René Echevarria, Brannon Braga, Naren Shankar

title: Blaze of Glory
description: Driven to desperation by the Dominion, the Maquis launch a massive bio-weapons strike towards Cardassia. Sisko must trust the traitor Eddington to try to stop them.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe, David Weddle

title: Blink of an Eye
description: Voyager becomes trapped in orbit of a planet where time passes rapidly, days within seconds, and enters the mythos of its indigenous people.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Michael Taylor

title: Bliss
description: The Voyager crew discovers what seems to be a wormhole leading to the Alpha Quadrant and home. Images of Earth and letters from home elates the crew of Voyager. Seven, and others, however, are skeptical of this seeming deliverance.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Doherty, Bill Prady

title: Blood Fever
description: Ensign Vorik's attempt to make B'Elanna his mate during Pon farr biochemically destabilizes B'Elanna, heightening her own aggressive emotions towards taking a mate.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink

title: Blood Oath
description: Three legendary Klingons come to DS9 to see if Jadzia will participate in a blood oath that they made with Dax's previous host Curzon.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Peter Allan Fields, Andrea Moore Alton

title: Bloodlines
description: Picard races the Ferengi in an effort to track down the son he never knew he had.
writer: Nick Sagan, René Echevarria, Naren Shankar

title: Body Parts
description: Odo is suddenly struck by illness. He is barely able to hold shape. Bashir and Odo see no other alternative than going to the Founders. Garak wants to come along.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe, George A. Brozak

title: Body and Soul
description: To circumvent The Doctor's obliteration by photon-hating humanoids, Seven secretly becomes his bodily host. In turn, The Doctor's personality dominates.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Eric Morris, Phyllis Strong

title: Booby Trap
description: The Enterprise triggers an ancient yet effective trap left by an extinct race, with a perfectly preserved derelict ship serving as cheese for Picard.
writer: Ron Roman, Richard Danus, Michael I. Wagner

title: Borderland
description: After a Klingon ship is attacked, it's discovered the imprisoned Dr. Soong's genetically enhanced humans are responsible. Archer takes him to prevent a war.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ken LaZebnik

title: Bound
description: The men of Enterprise fall under the seductive control of three Orion women. Trip is the only male member of the crew not to succumb to the women.
writer: Gene Roddenberry

title: Bounty
description: After exploring an uninhabited planet, Captain Archer is taken prisoner by a Tellarite bounty hunter who was hired by the Klingons to return Archer to Qo'noS.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Tobeason, Michael Sussman

title: Bread and Circuses
description: Kirk and the Enterprise computer become detectives after Scotty is accused of murdering women on a pleasure planet.
writer: Robert Bloch

title: Breaking the Ice
description: Enterprise discovers a comet which contains the very rare element eisilium. Then unexpectedly a Vulcan ship arrives. Captain Vanik tells they're interested in human behavior.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, André Jacquemetton, Maria Jacquemetton

title: Bride of Chaotica!
description: The Voyager crew encounters photonic life forms that mistake warmongering characters as real in Tom Paris' far-fetched "Captain Proton" holodeck program.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bryan Fuller, Michael Taylor

title: Broken Bow
description: In the mid-22nd century, the Earth ship Enterprise is launched under the command of Captain Jonathan Archer. When the crew rescues an alien from a crashed spaceship, Earth gets its first look at the alien's race - the Klingons. Archer and his crew must walk a fine line as they attempt to communicate with the Klingon pilot, whose language is completely unknown, and whose homeworld wants him back. Is this the beginning of friendship...or interstellar war?
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Rick Berman

title: Broken Pieces
description: Picard realizes how far some will go to protect secrets that go back generations when truths about the attack on Mars are revealed; Narissa orders her guards to capture Elnor, setting off a chain reaction on the Borg cube.

title: Brothers
description: After an accident aboard the Enterprise leaves one of its children in grave danger, Data commandeers the Enterprise, driven to take the ship to an unknown origin, where an interesting figure awaits.
writer: Rick Berman, Ronald D. Moore, Joe Menosky

title: Business as Usual
description: Quark must wrestle with his conscience when he becomes involved with arms merchants.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bradley Thompson, David Weddle

title: By Any Other Name
description: Looking for a missing Federation cultural observer, Kirk and Spock find themselves on a planet whose culture has been completely patterned after Nazi Germany.
writer: John Meredyth Lucas

title: By Inferno's Light
description: The station readies for a Dominion attack. Worf and Garak meet some unexpected friends in a Dominion Prison camp.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe, David Weddle

title: Call to Arms
description: Deep Space Nine prepares for a Dominion attack.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe, David Weddle

title: Canamar
description: Archer and Trip are accused of smuggling and sent to the penal colony Canamar. While Enterprise manages to negotiate their release, a mutiny breaks out on the prison ship.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, André Bormanis, John Shiban

title: Captain's Holiday
description: After mediating a difficult trade agreement, Captain Picard is encouraged to take a much needed rest on a vacationing planet, where he's visited by a strange race from the future, in search of a dangerous weapon.
writer: Ira Steven Behr, Ronald D. Moore

title: Captive Pursuit
description: For the first time an alien comes from the other side of the wormhole. He has ship trouble and seems very reluctant to accept any help. Chief O'Brien quickly finds out he has a secret.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Jill Donner

title: Carbon Creek
description: On the first anniversary of her assignment to Enterprise, T'Pol tells Archer and Trip about the first contact between humans and Vulcans, which involves three Vulcans becoming stranded in the town of Carbon Creek, Pennsylvania in 1957.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Chris Black, Rick Berman

title: Cardassians
description: Political tensions rise on DS9 when Bajorans board the station with a supposedly orphaned Cardassian whom they have adopted.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, James Crocker, Gene Wolande

title: Caretaker
description: While pursuing the trail of Maquis rebels, a newly commissioned Starfleet ship gets pulled to the far side of the galaxy.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Piller, Jeri Taylor

title: Carpenter Street
description: Archer and T'Pol travel back in time to Detroit in 2004 to stop three Xindi-Reptilians from constructing a bioweapon.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Rick Berman, André Bormanis

title: Cathexis
description: In an attempt to develop a cure for the Phage, a Vidiian doctor captures Torres and splits her into her 2 halves, one human - the other, Klingon.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Jonathan Glassner

title: Catspaw
description: In the throes of his Pon Farr mating period, Spock must return to Vulcan to meet his intended future wife, betrothed from childhood.
writer: Theodore Sturgeon

title: Cause And Effect
description: The Enterprise gets caught in a time loop which always has one result: total destruction of the ship itself.
writer: Brannon Braga, René Echevarria

title: Cease Fire
description: Captain Archer is asked to negotiate between the Andorians and Vulcans who are fighting over a small planet.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Chris Black, André Bormanis, Connections, Treksperts Briefing Room: Star Trek: Enterprise Writers Room Reunion (Live at STLV)

title: Chain Of Command, Part I
description: Picard is replaced as captain of the Enterprise so he, Lt. Worf and Dr. Crusher go on a top-secret mission into Cardassian space. Meanwhile, his replacement, Captain Jellico, meets his new command with some resistance from the crew.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, Frank Abatemarco, Brannon Braga

title: Chain Of Command, Part II
description: While the humorless Captain Jellico leads the endeavor to prevent a Cardassian invasion, Picard is captured and tortured by a ruthless interrogator in an attempt to break him.
writer: Frank Abatemarco, Brannon Braga, René Echevarria

title: Change of Heart
description: Worf and Jadzia travel to the badlands to procure some information on the Dominion from a traitorous Cardassian. Bashir wants to engage in a holo-suite spy simulation, but O'Brien prefers to brush up on his tongo game after watching Jadzia lose to Quark.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, David Weddle

title: Charlie X
description: Nurse Chapel is reunited with her fiancé; but his new obsession leads him to make an android duplicate of Captain Kirk.
writer: Robert Bloch

title: Child's Play
description: When Icheb's parents are located, Seven of Nine should be overjoyed, but instead does not believe their tale of Icheb's original assimilation.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Raf Green, Paul Brown

title: Children of Time
description: The DS9 crew discovers a small colony on a remote world that was originally populated by them 200 years earlier due to a time-travel accident with the Defiant. The current colonists lives depend on making sure that accident happens again, even though they've now forwarned the crew that it will happen. Each crew member needs to decide if they are willing to give up their lives on DS9 in order to preserve the history of the colony.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, Gary Holland

title: Children of the Comet
description: An ancient alien relic thwarts the Enterprise crew from re-routing a comet on track to strike an inhabited planet.

title: Chimera
description: After searching out and finding Odo, another changeling asks him to leave DS9 with him, even though the rest of the crew aren't convinced the new changeling is not part of the Founders.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, David Weddle

title: Chosen Realm
description: Enterprise is taken over by religious zealots who want to use the ship in a war against heretics.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Manny Coto, André Bormanis

title: Chrysalis
description: Dr. Bashir attempts to bring one of his genetically engineered friends out of the catatonic state she is in, and when he is successful, he begins to fall for her.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, David Weddle

title: Civil Defense
description: The station goes into lockdown and the crew is endangered when an old Cardassian security program is accidentally activated.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Mike Krohn, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Civilization
description: T'Pol finds unusual neutrino readings on a planet with a preindustrial society.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman, Phyllis Strong

title: Clues
description: Effects of passing through a wormhole give the Enterprise crew a sense that they were unconscious for more than the thirty seconds they've been led to believe.
writer: Bruce D. Arthurs, Joe Menosky, Ronald D. Moore

title: Coda
description: Chakotay and Janeway crash on a planet and are stuck in a time loop where Janeway dies.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink

title: Code of Honor
description: The Enterprise makes a confrontational first contact with the piratical Ferengi race as their two ships are ensnared by a presumed dead planet ready to judge them.
writer: Herbert Wright, Richard Krzemien

title: Cogenitor
description: Enterprise encounters a hyper-giant star. While there, they make first contact with the Vissians, a technologically sophisticated race with three genders. While making quick friends, and eagerly learning about the advanced technology, Trip gets curious about the Vissians third gender, known as the Cogenitor, who is crucial in the Vissian reproductive process. Against the wishes of the Vissians, Trip befriends the Cogenitor and encourages it to defy its cultural boundaries, which the Vissians are made aware of, and are angered by.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Rick Berman, André Bormanis

title: Cold Fire
description: The Caretaker's remains resonate, which might well mean that its mate is nearby. The trail leads to a space colony of Ocampa with psychokinetic powers.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Anthony Williams

title: Cold Front
description: Enterprise has a group of aliens watching a stellar phenomenon on board. When the ship is miraculously saved from blowing up, crewman Daniels has an important message for Archer.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Stephen Beck, Tim Finch

title: Cold Station 12
description: The Augments are on their way to Cold Station 12, where Augments embryos are kept.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Alan Brennert

title: Collective
description: Several Borg children abduct Chakotay, Kim, Neelix and Paris.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Taylor, Andrew Price

title: Coming of Age
description: The Enterprise searches for answers as to why three Klingon warriors were the only survivors aboard a freighter just inside the Neutral Zone which was seemingly attacked by a Ferengi ship.
writer: Herbert Wright, D.C. Fontana, Hans Beimler

title: Concerning Flight
description: After aliens raid Voyager, Janeway discovers her Leonardo holodeck character living independently at a trading post under the patronage of a shameless "prince" of thieves.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Jimmy Diggs

title: Conspiracy
description: While assigned to investigate missing outposts in the Neutral Zone, the Enterprise revives three cryogenically frozen people found aboard a wayward derelict Earth vessel.
writer: Deborah McIntyre, Mona Clee, Hannah Louise Shearer

title: Contagion
description: Encountering the superior technology of a very ancient race creates system malfunctions for a doomed Federation starship, for the Enterprise, and for an eavesdropping Romulan warbird.
writer: Steve Gerber, Beth Woods, Leonard Mlodinow

title: Conundrum
description: When an approaching alien ship wipes their memory, the crew struggles to put back together what happened.
writer: Barry M. Schkolnick, Paul Schiffer, Brannon Braga

title: Cost Of Living
description: As undetectable parasites devour the ship, Deanna's mother arrives, taking young Alexander under her wing en route to meeting a man she's blindly agreed to marry.
writer: Peter Allan Fields, Brannon Braga, René Echevarria

title: Countdown
description: While the Reptilians force Hoshi to decipher a launch code, the Primates, Arboreals and Archer think of a way to stop the Xindi weapon.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, André Bormanis, Chris Black

title: Counterpoint
description: Voyager passes through a sector of space controlled by a race that is deeply suspicious of telepathic lifeforms. The presence of Tuvok and other telepaths forces the Voyager crew to develop a novel approach in order to pass through safely.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Taylor, Bryan Fuller

title: Course: Oblivion
description: As unseen aliens adjust his neural pathways, Chakotay recalls both boxing at the academy under groundskeeper Boothby's management and watching after his "crazy old" grandfather.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Michael Taylor

title: Court Martial
description: The Enterprise must decide on its response when a Romulan ship makes a destructively hostile armed probe of Federation territory.
writer: Paul Schneider

title: Covenant
description: Kira is kidnapped and taken to Empok Nor by a member of a Pah'Wraith cult and discovers the group leader is Gul Dukat.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, David Weddle

title: Crisis Point
description: Mariner repurposes Boimler's holodeck program to cast herself as the villain in a Lower Decks style movie.

title: Crisis Point 2: Paradoxus
description: Boimler's holodeck movie sequel tries to live up to the original.

title: Critical Care
description: Stolen and sold off, The Doctor winds up enslaved at an alien hospital, where social status strictly defines the quality of provided health care.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, James Kahn, Robert Doherty

title: Crossfire
description: Dukat returns, stripped of his prestige, to escort Kira to a Cardassian conference about the Dominion. But an attack by a Klingon raider may give Dukat a chance to redeem himself in the eyes of his government.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, Tom Benko

title: Crossover
description: Kira and Bashir find themselves in an alternate universe.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Peter Allan Fields, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Crossroads
description: When the crew attempts to secure transport to the Federation, they unwittingly cross paths with the Vice Admiral who is hunting them.

title: Cupid's Errant Arrow
description: Mariner is suspicious of Boimler's new girlfriend. Tendi and Rutherford grow jealous of a bigger starship's gear.

title: Daedalus
description: Dr. Emory Erickson comes aboard to supposedly conduct tests for subquantum transportation. However, he seems to have a different agenda.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Alan Brennert, Ken LaZebnik

title: Dagger of the Mind
description: After the Enterprise is forced to destroy a dangerous marker buoy, a gigantic alien ship arrives to capture and condemn the crew as trespassers.
writer: Jerry Sohl

title: Damage
description: Captain Archer has been returned to Enterprise by the Xindi council awaiting a hearing. But after a heavy attack by the Reptilians, Enterprise is stranded without a functional warp drive. They meet another ship in trouble because of space anomalies. The ship's captain isn't willing to trade in their warp coil and Archer must make a tough decision. Meanwhile T'Pol is having some strange sensations and can't control her emotions.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Phyllis Strong, André Bormanis

title: Dark Frontier
description: Aboard the Delta Flyer, Janeway leads Tuvok, Paris and the Doctor on a rescue mission to retrieve Seven from the Borg Queen. whose treatment of Seven is markedly atypical.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Bryan Fuller

title: Dark Page
description: While establishing diplomatic relations with an alien race called the Cairn, Deanna's mother experiences a mental breakdown and falls into a coma, and it seems like Deanna is the only one who is able to help her, even if her mother doesn't want it.
writer: Hilary Bader, René Echevarria, Naren Shankar

title: Darkling
description: As Kes develops a relationship with a Mikhal Traveler, a member of Voyager's crew develops a dangerously ruthless split personality with a fixation on Kes.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Brannon Braga

title: Darmok
description: Picard must learn to communicate with a race that speaks in metaphor under a difficult set of circumstances.
writer: Joe Menosky, Philip LaZebnik, Brannon Braga

title: Data's Day
description: Data tries to comprehend the complex emotions between O'Brien and Keiko, who are about to be married.
writer: Harold Apter, Ronald D. Moore, Joe Menosky

title: Datalore
description: Riker, Troi, Data and Yar beam down to a planet ruled by a matriarchal government in the hopes of locating a missing freighter crew.
writer: Patrick Barry, Sandy Fries, Tracy Tormé

title: Dawn
description: Trip's shuttlepod is shot down by a hostile race and is forced to land on a deserted moon. There he must team up with the alien who shot him down in order to find a way back to Enterprise.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, John Shiban, André Bormanis

title: Dax
description: Jadzia Dax is accused of a murder that her previous host, Curzon, supposedly committed.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, D.C. Fontana, Peter Allan Fields

title: Day of Honor
description: Needy alien refugees, who look upon Voyager's generosity as stingy, put Torres and Paris in a life-threatening situation during a crossroads in their relationship.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink, Bryan Fuller

title: Day of the Dove
description: A group of aliens who exist in a state of incredible acceleration invade the Enterprise and abduct Capt. Kirk.
writer: Arthur Heinemann, Gene L. Coon, Arthur H. Singer

title: Dead Stop
description: Enterprise seeks repairs from their encounters with the Romulans, where they stop at a desolate but idyllic repair station. But all is not what it seems when Mayweather is suddenly found dead.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman, Phyllis Strong, Connections, Treksperts Briefing Room: Star Trek: Enterprise Writers Room Reunion (Live at STLV)

title: Deadlock
description: A schism duplicates everything in Voyager; except the antimatter, which both ships share. As one ship rips apart, the other comes under attack from organ-harvesting Vidiians.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Lisa Klink

title: Dear Doctor
description: Phlox is asked to save the Valakians from annihilation by disease. However, he discovers something unusual about the Menk, another humanoid race on the planet.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, André Jacquemetton, Maria Jacquemetton

title: Death Wish
description: As Voyager's captain, Janeway conducts a hearing for asylum tied to a suicidal Q's right to die.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Shawn Piller, Kenneth Biller

title: Defiant
description: Riker arrives on the station to requisition the Defiant in order to fight with the Maquis. Sisko assists Gul Dukat on Cardassia Prime to prevent the ship from entering Cardassian territory.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Deja Q
description: Much to Picard's displeasure, Q reappears on the Enterprise, claiming to have been ejected from the Q Continuum, and therefore, lost his powers.
writer: Richard Danus, Ronald D. Moore

title: Demon
description: Critically low on energy, Voyager limps to a hellish Y-class "Demon" planet for much needed deuterium. Harry and Tom beam down and make accidental contact with a gooey life form.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, André Bormanis

title: Demons
description: While attending a conference on a proposed interplanetary alliance, Trip and T'Pol find out from a dying woman that they have a baby. Investigation shows the woman was a member of the xenophobic organization Terra Prime.
writer: Gene Roddenberry

title: Descent
description: When Data experiences his first emotion, anger, after an encounter with the Borg, Data tries hard to recreate the experience, and he is blind as to what the consequences will be. Meanwhile, the rest of the Enterprise investigate the mystery as to why the Borg they faced seemed to have names and feelings of their own.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, Jeri Taylor, Brannon Braga

title: Descent, Part II
description: Before Lore can run risky experiments on Geordi, Troi and Picard, the three seek a way to re-boot Data's ethical program that Lore switched off to pull Data to his side.
writer: René Echevarria, Naren Shankar

title: Desert Crossing
description: Enterprise is for the second time on its way to Risa when yet again another urgent matter comes up: a distress call. It comes from a small vessel. The owner, Zobral, is extremely happy with the help from Enterprise and invites Captain Archer to his home world for a good meal and a game of Geskana. When Archer and Trip have landed on the desert planet, Enterprise gets a message from the Torothan Chancellor Trelit. He says Zobral isn't as nice a man as captain Archer thinks.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, André Bormanis, Rick Berman

title: Destiny
description: Sisko faces a conflict between his Starfleet duties and his role as the Bajoran Emissary when an ancient Bajoran prophecy predicts a joint Federation-Cardassian project will lead to the destruction of the wormhole.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, David S. Cohen, Martin A. Winer

title: Detained
description: Archer and Mayweather are captured by the Tandarans and placed in a Suliban internment camp.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman, Phyllis Strong

title: Devil's Due
description: While answering a distress call, Picard finds himself not sure of exactly who he is dealing with, but he's sure he's not dealing with the planet's version of the Devil, as she claims to be.
writer: Philip LaZebnik, William Douglas Lansford, Ronald D. Moore

title: Disaster
description: Collision with a quantum filament leaves Troi in charge of a damaged and endangered Enterprise with emergency bulkheads cutting off sections of the ship.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, Ron Jarvis, Philip A. Scorza

title: Disengage
description: Aided by crew of the U.S.S. Titan, Picard makes a discovery that will alter his life forever and puts him on a collision course with the most cunning enemy he's ever encountered.

title: Displaced
description: Voyager's crew is being replaced by strangers one by one and the replacements don't know why they are there.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink

title: Distant Origin
description: A superior race, descended from Earth dinosaurs, discovers Voyager, but living proof of the controversial Distant Origin Theory goes against widespread doctrine.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Joe Menosky

title: Distant Voices
description: After being attacked in the infirmary, Dr. Bashir has a dual mystery on his hands: what has happened to the station while he was out and why is he aging so rapidly now?
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe, Joe Menosky

title: Divergence
description: While Enterprise tries to bring Trip on board to reverse the malicious Klingon modifications, Phlox and Antaak find a cure for the virus that will not please General K'Vagh.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Garfield Reeves-Stevens, Judith Reeves-Stevens

title: Doctor Bashir, I Presume
description: Dr. Bashir is selected to be the model for the next-generation of Emergency Medical Hologram (EMH). But he balks when his parents are invited to the station to be interviewed by the EMH programmer, Dr. Zimmerman. There is a dark secret in his family that he's afraid that his parents will reveal.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, Jimmy Diggs

title: Doctor's Orders
description: While in command of Enterprise because the entire crew is in a coma, Dr. Phlox starts hearing some strange sounds.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Chris Black, André Bormanis

title: Dominion
description: Crippled, cornered, and out of options, Picard stages a gambit to trap Vadic and reveal her true motive - a gamble that puts the Titan in the crosshairs and forces Picard and Beverly to question every moral code they've ever held.

title: Dragon's Teeth
description: An encounter with aliens in a subspace corridor leads Voyager to take refuge on a radioactive planet with remnants of its civilization tucked underground in stasis chambers.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Taylor, Joe Menosky

title: Dramatis Personae
description: The entire station's command staff, save Odo, become power hungry and vie to defeat each other by forming secret alliances and plotting assassinations. Odo tries to figure out the cause of this mysterious behavior.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Dreadnought
description: B'Elanna confronts her Maquis past when Voyager discovers a Cardassian super-weapon in the Delta Quadrant with artificial intelligence that B'Elanna once modified.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Gary Holland, Lisa Klink

title: Dream Catcher
description: The crew has their first away mission on an undiscovered planet that manifests their deepest desires, only to realize the planet has desires of its own.

title: Drive
description: Tom's sudden entry in an alien spacecraft race throws a wrench into B'Elanna's labored plans for a romantic holodeck getaway for two, threatening their relationship.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Taylor, Robert Doherty

title: Drone
description: Voyager investigates the birth of a nebula. Unfortunately, its intense blast wave catches an away mission shuttle, causing emergency beam out transporter signals to fuse the Doctor's mobile emitter with Seven's nanoprobes. The mobile emitter subsequently starts assimilating a science lab and assaults a crew member to create a new Borg drone built upon the emitter's twenty-ninth century technology.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bryan Fuller, Joe Menosky

title: Duet
description: A Cardassian suffering from Kalla-Nohra, a disease that indicates he served in a labor camp, visits DS9. Kira is determined to convict him as a war criminal.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Peter Allan Fields, Lisa Rich

title: Elaan of Troyius
description: An apparently insane Capt. Kirk has the Enterprise deliberately enter the Romulan Neutral Zone where the ship is immediately captured by the enemy.
writer: D.C. Fontana, Arthur H. Singer

title: Elementary, Dear Data
description: An attempt to provide Data with a challenging Sherlock Holmes holodeck program backfires when its Professor Moriarty character accidentally becomes self-aware.
writer: Brian Alan Lane, Arthur Conan Doyle

title: Elogium
description: The Voyager encounters a swarm of mysterious life forms that has a strange effect on the ship's key operating system and whose presence accelerates Kes' reproductive cycle.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Jimmy Diggs

title: Emanations
description: The crew encounters a hedonistic alien race with the ability to travel through the galaxy at will -- an ability that may be the key to Voyager's returning home.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Perricone, Greg Elliot

title: Emergence
description: Strange images begin appearing throughout the ship's decks and holodecks. Data and the rest of the crew begin to investigate only to discover that the ship's computer itself is exhibiting the strange behavior as it begins to become self-aware.
writer: Joe Menosky, Brannon Braga, René Echevarria

title: Emissary
description: When the troubled Commander Sisko takes command of a surrendered space station, he learns that it borders a unique stable wormhole.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Piller, Rick Berman

title: Empok Nor
description: While salvaging components from DS9's sister station, the crew run afoul of a "surprise" left behind by the Cardassians.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, Bryan Fuller

title: Encounter at Farpoint
description: On the maiden mission of the U.S.S. Enterprise (NCC-1701-D), an omnipotent being known as Q challenges the crew to discover the secret of a mysterious base in an advanced and civilized fashion.
writer: D.C. Fontana

title: Ensign Ro
description: Captain Picard foils a plot against the Bajorans with his new Bajoran officer, Ensign Ro.
writer: Rick Berman, Brannon Braga, René Echevarria

title: Envoys
description: After a high-profile mission goes awry, Boimler is further plagued with self-doubt when Mariner proves herself to be a more naturally talented sci-fi badass than he. Rutherford quits his job in engineering and explores other departments on the U.S.S. Cerritos.

title: Equilibrium
description: The Trill homeworld is the setting, as Sisko and Bashir take Jadzia home for treatment when she begins experiencing hallucinations after playing a tune she can't recall.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, Christopher Teague

title: Equinox, Part II
description: While trying to run down the Equinox and retrieve its captain, Chakotay fears Janeway becoming equally as unethical and corrupt, challenging his loyalty.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Rick Berman

title: Errand of Mercy
description: Existence itself comes under threat from a man's power-struggle with his alternate self, with the Enterprise's strained dilithium crystals presenting his key to a final solution.
writer: Don Ingalls

title: Et in Arcadia Ego, Part 1
description: With the Romulans in pursuit, Picard and the crew finally reach Soji's home planet - and discover more than they expected about the inhabitants.

title: Et in Arcadia Ego, Part 2
description: Picard and his team are pitted against the Romulans and the synthetics of Coppelius in a final confrontation.

title: Ethics
description: After an accident that leaves him no longer able to walk, Worf asks Riker to help him commit suicide.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, Sara B Cooper, Stuart Charno

title: Evolution
description: Wesley's latest science project escapes the lab, threatening the Enterprise and an eminent scientist's life-long project.
writer: Michael Piller

title: Ex Post Facto
description: When the crew finds a mysterious alien burial ground within an asteroid, a unknown phenomenon that teleports the bodies suddenly transports Kim to another planet where an alien race believes he is a being from the afterlife.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Kenneth Biller

title: Exile
description: Hoshi Sato is contacted by a 400-year-old telepathic alien exiled on planet in the Expanse, shunned by his society because of his abilities. The alien offers to use his powers to help Enterprise find the Xindi and the weapon they are building, but only if Hoshi will stay with him while he works. She agrees to stay behind, while Enterprise confirms the Expanse has a second Sphere and is on its way to investigate.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Phyllis Strong, André Bormanis

title: Explorers
description: Sisko rebuilds an ancient Bajoran space vessel from the blueprints, and he and Jake take the ship on a trip, attempting to prove that the ancient Bajorans went beyond their solar system without warp drive.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, Hilary Bader

title: Extinction
description: Archer, Reed and Sato are transformed into aliens while visiting a planet.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, André Bormanis, Jonathan Fernandez

title: Extreme Measures
description: Dr. Bashir and Chief O'Brien go to some pretty extreme measures to try and track down a cure for Odo.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bradley Thompson, David Weddle

title: Extreme Risk
description: B'Elanna Torres secretly participates in several dangerous holodeck programs. The Voyager crew works around the clock to build the Delta Flyer, hoping to beat the Malon in retrieving a lost probe from the atmosphere of a gas giant.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Bryan Fuller

title: Eye of the Beholder
description: As a young lieutenant aboard the Enterprise commits suicide, his shipmates deal with the loss and Deanna deals with feelings surrounding her as she and Worf begin to form a relationship.
writer: René Echevarria, Brannon Braga, Naren Shankar

title: Eye of the Needle
description: Tuvok plays detective when Paris is wrongfully convicted by an alien race of murder.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Evan Somers, Kenneth Biller

title: E²
description: Trying to pass through a subspace corridor, Enterprise is stopped by a version of itself from the future.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman, André Bormanis

title: Face Of The Enemy
description: Deanna awakes to find herself altered to look like a Romulan, immediately unaware she's involved in a cat-and-mouse game as a member of their intelligence agency.
writer: Naren Shankar, René Echevarria, Brannon Braga

title: Faces
description: Neelix is diagnosed with a fatal illness by a Haakonian named Jetrel. The same man who'd developed a doomsday weapon which destroyed a Talaxian moon and killed Neelix's family.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Jack Klein, Karen Klein

title: Facets
description: Dax asks the crew to join her Zhian'tara ritual. The memories of her former hosts will take over their bodies. Nog is preparing for Starfleet Academy.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Fair Haven
description: Capt. Janeway falls for an Irish barman character she modifies within Tom's holodeck Irish village recreational getaway for the crew called Fair Haven.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robin Bernheim, Robert Doherty

title: Fair Trade
description: Voyager heads into a section of space that Neelix has no knowledge of, so he tries to obtain a map of it from a less-than-reputable friend.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, André Bormanis, Ronald Wilkerson

title: Fallen Hero
description: Enterprise is planning a trip to the relaxation planet Risa, when it gets an urgent request from the Vulcan High Command. They must pick up ambassador V'Lar who has fallen in disgrace. Apparently she misused her position during negotiations on the planet Mazar and has been evicted. After Enterprise picked up V'Lar and is on its way for a meeting with the Vulcan warship Sh'Ran, suddenly a Mazarite ship appears. They want V'Lar for further interrogating. When captain Archer wants to discuss it first with Starfleet Command, they open fire.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Alan Cross, Rick Berman

title: False Profits
description: Voyager comes across a pair of Alpha Quadrant Ferengis who have set themselves up as gods on a primitive planet. Voyager wants to end their reign and use the nearby wormhole to return home.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, George A. Brozak

title: Family
description: After defeating the Borg, the crew of the Enterprise experiences shore leave in various ways. Captain Picard's return to his family's vineyard in France has some extraordinary repercussions.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, Susanne Lambdin, Bryan Stewart

title: Family Business
description: Quark and Rom return to the Ferengi home world because their mother has broken the law - by wearing clothes and earning profits, forbidden for women in Ferengi society.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Far Beyond the Stars
description: Captain Sisko has a full sensory vision of himself as an under-appreciated science fiction magazine writer in 1950s America.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, Marc Scott Zicree

title: Farewell
description: With just hours until the Europa Launch, Picard and the crew find themselves in a race against time to save the future.

title: Fascination
description: When Lwaxana Troi visits the station for the Bajoran Gratitude Festival, the crew is stricken with intense romantic attractions to one another.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Philip LaZebnik, James Crocker

title: Favor the Bold
description: Sisko mounts an attack to re-take DS9, while Rom faces charges as an enemy of the Dominion state.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, David Weddle

title: Favorite Son
description: Ensign Kim's DNA changes, bringing with it memories of the region while a planet of alien women claim him as a favorite son returning home to mate.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink

title: Ferengi Love Songs
description: Quark doesn't know whether to be panicked or overjoyed when he learns that his mother is the new lover of the Grand Nagus.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, David Weddle

title: Field of Fire
description: To find an apparently untraceable murderer, Ezri Dax reluctantly calls up the residual consciousness remnant of her symbiont's past hidden psychopathic murderer host, Joran.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe, David Weddle

title: Fight or Flight
description: Captain Archer wants to convert curiosity into deeds and decides to enter a ship floating in space. Hoshi has trouble adjusting to life on Enterprise.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Rick Berman, Phyllis Strong

title: Final Mission
description: Before leaving for Starfleet Academy, Wesley Crusher accompanies Captain Picard on a dangerous mission.
writer: Kacey Arnold-Ince, Jeri Taylor, Ronald D. Moore

title: First Con-tact
description: When a mentor from Dal's past persuades him to use their Federation cover for personal gain, they quickly discover Starfleet has protocols for a reason.

title: First Contact
description: Disguised as an alien prior to First Contact, Will's life becomes imperiled when incurred injuries reveal his foreign internal structure to a xenophobic alien population.
writer: Dennis Bailey, David Bischoff, Joe Menosky

title: First First Contact
description: The U.S.S. Cerritos is tasked to aid an Excelsior-class starship on a first contact mission.

title: First Flight
description: After his close friend and colleague A.G. Robinson is killed in a mountain climbing accident, Captain Archer tells T'Pol about the Warp 2 test flight almost ten years earlier.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, John Shiban, Chris Black

title: Firstborn
description: In an effort to help him accept his Klingon heritage, Worf and his son, Alexander, attend an ancient Klingon ceremony.
writer: René Echevarria, Mark Kalbfeld, Naren Shankar

title: Flashback
description: Captain Janeway participates in a mind meld with Tuvok, who relives his experiences on the U.S.S. Excelsior under the command of Captain Sulu at the time ofStar Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country (1991).
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Lisa Klink

title: Flesh and Blood
description: Free from their pursuers, the leader of the holograms decides to continue the crusade against the organics in order to liberate all holograms, everywhere. The Doctor finally realises what he had done and comes up with a plan to redeem himself.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bryan Fuller, Jack Monaco

title: Fly Me to the Moon
description: Picard discovers an important person from his past, Q continues his manipulation of the timeline, taking an interest in Dr. Adam Soong. Seven and Raffi attempt a daring rescue of Rios.

title: For the Cause
description: A renegade group of Jem'Hadar plunders Deep Space Nine. Sisko agrees to a combat operation with loyal Jem'Hadar to prevent the renegades completing a planetary gateway.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: For the Uniform
description: When the traitorous Eddington returns, Sisko will go to any lengths to capture him.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Peter Allan Fields, David Weddle

title: For the World is Hollow and I Have Touched the Sky
description: After Dr. McCoy helps the leader of a planet populated by people with powerful psionic abilities, they decide to force him to stay by torturing his comrades until he submits.
writer: Meyer Dolinsky, Arthur H. Singer

title: Force of Nature
description: While searching for a missing Federation medical transport, the Enterprise is accosted by two scientists from a nearby planet who claim that cumulative exposure to warp energy is weakening the fabric of space.
writer: Naren Shankar, René Echevarria

title: Fortunate Son
description: Enterprise is sent to help the freighter Fortunate after an attack. However, its first officer, Ryan, is hiding something.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, James Duff, Phyllis Strong

title: Frame of Mind
description: Riker begins to question reality when he finds himself in an alien insane asylum and faces the prospect his life on the Enterprise has been a delusion.
writer: Brannon Braga, René Echevarria, Naren Shankar

title: Friday's Child
description: A powerful artificially intelligent Earth probe, with a murderously twisted imperative, comes aboard the Enterprise and mistakes Capt. Kirk for its creator.
writer: John Meredyth Lucas

title: Friendship One
description: Chakotay and Seven of Nine's shuttle is crippled by an energy field and they crash on a planet inhabited by a primitive species.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, James Kahn, Michael Taylor

title: Fury
description: An incensed Kes returns to Voyager to travel back in time and abduct her younger self, inadvertently causing younger Tuvok to experience precognitive hallucinations.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Taylor, Bryan Fuller

title: Fusion
description: When Enterprise encounters a ship of Vulcan pilgrims, T'Pol is convinced by one of them to perform a Vulcan mind meld.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Phyllis Strong, Michael Sussman

title: Future Imperfect
description: Riker awakens sixteen years after an away mission where he contracted a disease which destroyed his memory back to the point of infection - or so he's told.
writer: J. Larry Carroll, David Carren, Ronald D. Moore

title: Future Tense
description: Enterprise discovers a pod with the body of a human that seems to be wanted by both Sulibans and Tholians.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman, Phyllis Strong

title: Future's End
description: A timeship from the future who tries to stop Voyager gets thrown with Voyager into the twentieth century. His timeship is found in 1967 and Voyager discovers that a company that has benefited from its technology exists in 1996.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Joe Menosky

title: Future's End, Part II
description: As the Voyager crew pit their 24th century technology against Starling's stolen 29th century technology, Chakotay and Torres fall into the hands of paranoid white supremacists.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Joe Menosky

title: Galaxy's Child
description: As Geordi La Forge finally meets the real Dr. Leah Brahms, a tragic first contact leaves the Enterprise with a gigantic galactic infant suckling the ship's energy.
writer: Maurice Hurley, Tom Kartozian, Ronald D. Moore

title: Gambit, Part I
description: While investigating the apparent death of Capt. Picard, Riker is abducted by a group of intergalactic archaeological thieves, only to find Picard has apparently joined their ranks.
writer: Naren Shankar, Christopher Hatton, René Echevarria

title: Gambit, Part II
description: Riker and Picard must find out why this intergalactic gang is looting archaeological sites.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, Naren Shankar, René Echevarria

title: Genesis
description: When a new torpedo guidance system malfunctions, Picard and Data go chasing after it into an asteroid field, while the crew is left behind to deal with their own strange behaviors.
writer: Brannon Braga, René Echevarria, Naren Shankar

title: Ghost in the Machine
description: When the crew gets trapped in the holodeck by a mysterious malfunction, they aren't sure what's programmed or what's real.

title: Ghosts of Illyria
description: Una must confront a secret she's been hiding when a contagion ravages the ship, incapacitating the rest of the crew.

title: Good Shepherd
description: Three errant crewmen. What's Janeway to do? Take 'em on an away mission to see their colors true.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Dianna Gitto, Joe Menosky

title: Gravity
description: Stuck on a planet within a spatial "sinkhole," Tom pressures Tuvok to take an alien woman who fancies him for his own.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Nick Sagan, Bryan Fuller

title: Grounded
description: Mariner enlists her friends on a rogue mission to exonerate her mother as Captain Freeman faces a military tribunal for the destruction of Pakled Planet.

title: Half a Life
description: Counselor Troi's mother visits the Enterprise and becomes infatuated with a man whose culture forces him into a suicidal ritual called "The Resolution."
writer: Peter Allan Fields, Ted Roberts, Ronald D. Moore

title: Harbinger
description: With Reed and Hayes fighting over a training program and Trip seemingly making T'Pol jealous, Enterprise discovers a dying alien in a pod with technology similar to the Spheres.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Manny Coto, Rick Berman

title: Hard Time
description: When the Mirror Universe counterpart of Sisko's deceased wife lures Jake to the other side, Sisko must follow and help the Terran resistance against the Alliance forces.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler

title: Hatchery
description: Captain Archer orders the crew of Enterprise to save a Xindi-Insectoid hatchery.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, André Bormanis, Michael Sussman

title: Haven
description: Captain Picard and some of his crew are caught in a deadly trap in the holodeck as the result of a ship-wide scan from an alien race.
writer: Tracy Tormé

title: Hear All, Trust Nothing
description: The Cerritos crew unexpectedly spends a day on Deep Space 9.

title: Heart of Glory
description: The Enterprise encounters a planet long dead, but technical equipment still functions for the purpose of selling off its arsenal, along with self sufficiency.
writer: Richard Manning, Hans Beimler, Robert Lewin

title: Heart of Stone
description: After chasing a fugitive into a cave, Kira gets stuck and is slowly encapsulated by a growing crystal stone while Odo tries to figure out how to free her.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Hero Worship
description: Data rescues an orphaned boy named Timothy from a damaged ship. As a way to repress his own pain, Timothy begins to mimic Data's personality.
writer: Joe Menosky, Hilary Bader, Brannon Braga

title: Heroes and Demons
description: An encounter with a peculiar nebula suddenly leaves Chakotay brain dead and unconscious. The crew is left with a mysterious but powerful force of energy onboard that can take over the minds of the crew members.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Joe Menosky

title: Hide And Q
description: While on a mission to a planet called Haven, Counselor Troi meets her husband to be, a marriage arranged by her father years before, as the Enterprise encounters a ship far deadlier than any combat could provide.
writer: Tracy Tormé, Lan O'Kun

title: Hide and Seek
description: Picard must face the ghosts of his past when he and his crew are attacked by a new incarnation of an old enemy; Seven and Raffi face Jurati in a final showdown.

title: Hippocratic Oath
description: Kira and Gul Dukat go after the lost prison ship Ravinok. Dukat has a secret. Meanwhile Kasidy Yates tries to find work near Bajor, leaving Sisko uncomfortable.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Nicholas Corea, Toni Marberry

title: His Way
description: Odo is schooled in the ways of romance by a holographic programmed lounge singer.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, David Weddle

title: Hollow Pursuits
description: A shy member of the "Enterprise" crew becomes addicted to the holodeck, where relationships are easier than in real life, when his knowledge is needed in engineering.
writer: Sally Caves, Ronald D. Moore

title: Home
description: The crew take some time off. Archer meets an old friend, Captain Erika Hernandez. T'Pol takes Trip to Vulcan, with a surprise waiting. Phlox finds out Earth has become xenophobic.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman

title: Home Soil
description: Picard finds himself facing a strenuous test of his loyalty to Starfleet, one which the other members of the crew are struggling to cope with, whilst Wesley faces an equally challenging entrance exam at Starfleet Academy.
writer: Sandy Fries

title: Homefront
description: When Starfleet institutes martial law to combat the Changeling Menace, Sisko begins to question if the Dominion is the real threat.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe, Ronald D. Moore

title: Homestead
description: The Doctor abducts and impersonates various members of the crew when the Captain is kidnapped.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Phyllis Strong, Michael Sussman

title: Homeward
description: In an attempt to preserve a race of people on a planet being bombarded by storms that would kill them, Worf's foster brother violates the Prime Directive, leaving the Enterprise crew in a difficult position.
writer: Naren Shankar, Spike Steingasser, William N. Stape

title: Honor Among Thieves
description: Chief O'Brien goes undercover to infiltrate an Orion crime ring.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, Philip Kim

title: Hope and Fear
description: A tough coded message from Starfleet with a promise of home and a helpful alien adept at cracking language patterns. The coincidence seems too convenient for Capt. Janeway.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Joe Menosky

title: Horizon
description: Mayweather temporarily leaves Enterprise to visit the cargo ship Horizon, where he was born and raised. Meanwhile, the Enterprise crew observes an unusual planetary event.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, André Bormanis

title: How Sharper Than a Serpent's Tooth
description: The Enterprise runs into a being which once visited Earth and influenced the Mayan culture.

title: Human Error
description: When Q finds his rebellious son too much to handle, he brings him to Voyager in the hope that Captain Janeway can teach him responsibility and compassion.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Doherty, Michael Taylor

title: Hunters
description: Voyager starts to receive news from home both good and bad. They also encounter the Hirogen race who only live to hunt and have their sights on Voyager.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink, Bryan Fuller

title: I Borg
description: The Enterprise finds a lone Borg drone, separated from the collective, and brings him aboard. The drone begins to reassert his individuality, but his presence causes differing levels of fear and sympathy from various crew members.
writer: René Echevarria, Brannon Braga

title: I, Excretus
description: A consultant arrives on the U.S.S. Cerritos to run drills that require the lower deckers and bridge crew to swap duties.

title: I, Mudd
description: A landing party from the Enterprise is exposed to strange form of radiation which rapidly ages them.
writer: David P. Harmon

title: Identity Crisis
description: Lt. Commander Geordi La Forge and an old friend fight to defeat a parasite that is trying to transform them into another species.
writer: Brannon Braga, Tim de Haas, Ronald D. Moore

title: If Wishes Were Horses
description: While Dax investigates an unusual energy reading near DS9 to see if it's dangerous for the station, people's imaginations suddenly come alive, causing chaos.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Nell McCue Crawford, William Crawford

title: Image in the Sand
description: Having returned to Earth a couple of months ago, Sisko waits for the Prophets to instruct him on what to do next. When he receives one, he begins searching for the woman behind the face in the sand in his vision. Meanwhile, back on DS9, Kira deals with a promotion and handling the decisions surrounding her command, and finding herself wishing for the return of Sisko. And Worf deals with the loss of his wife, Jadzia Dax.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, David Weddle

title: Imaginary Friend
description: A powerful, potentially dangerous energy life form, intent on scouting out the Enterprise, steps into the role of a girl's imaginary friend.
writer: Edithe Swensen, Brannon Braga, Jean Louise Matthias

title: Imperfection
description: Seven of Nine's life is threatened when her cortical implants begin to degrade. Icheb wants to help her, but his proposition is very risky.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Carleton Eastlake, Robert Doherty

title: Imposters
description: Caught by Starfleet and facing court martial, paranoia grows as Picard struggles to uncover whether a prodigal crewman from his past has returned as an ally - or an enemy hellbent on destroying them all.

title: Improbable Cause
description: After an attempt is made on Garak's life, Odo conducts the investigation on who tried to murder the Cardassian tailor - and why.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, Robert Lederman

title: Impulse
description: Enterprise answers a distress signal from a stranded Vulcan ship, only when arriving they find the ship completely desolate, wrecked - and filled with Vulcan zombies.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Jonathan Fernandez, Terry Matalas

title: In Purgatory's Shadow
description: A coded message from the Gamma Quadrant leads Garak to believe his mentor, Enabran Tain, is still alive. He and Worf seek him out, only to discover something much worse: a Dominion invasion fleet poised to attack the Alpha Quadrant.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe, David Weddle

title: In Theory
description: When a female crew member is infatuated with Lieutenant Commander Data, he decides to give a romantic relationship a try. Also, the Enterprise finds itself having to maneuver through a dangerous nebula.
writer: Joe Menosky, Ronald D. Moore, David Carren

title: In a Mirror, Darkly
description: Set in the dark and oppressive Mirror Universe, Commander Archer leads a mutiny in a bold attempt to seize control of an advanced starship - a vessel from the future that may be the key to saving the Terran Empire.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman

title: In a Mirror, Darkly, Part II
description: The Mirror Universe saga concludes as Archer declares himself captain of a formidable starship from the future, while his alien crew launch a rebellion in a desperate bid to keep him from achieving ultimate power.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman

title: In the Cards
description: Jake and Nog go through hell and high water, from participating in an auction to dealing with the Dominion, to receiving a Willie Mays baseball card, all in order to cheer up the war-fatigued Captain Sisko.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, Truly Barr Clark

title: In the Flesh
description: The Voyager crew discovers Species 8472 training to pose as human beings in a recreation of Starfleet Headquarters, a prelude to infiltration.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Nick Sagan, Bryan Fuller

title: In the Hands of the Prophets
description: Vedek Winn, a candidate in the race for the open Kai position, stirs up trouble on DS9 when she attempts to boycott Keiko's school for not teaching Bajoran religious beliefs.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: In the Pale Moonlight
description: To save the Federation in a critical scheme, Sisko comes to realize that he must violate its fundamental principles to do so.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Taylor, Peter Allan Fields

title: Indiscretion
description: Lenara Kahn, the new host of the wife of Dax's former host Torias, comes to the station. While they're not allowed to renew their relation, there's still a spark.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, René Echevarria

title: Infinite Regress
description: Voyager comes in contact with Borg technology which causes Seven of Nine to display multiple personalities.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Doherty, Jimmy Diggs

title: Inheritance
description: On Federation planet Atrea IV, Data encounters the former wife of his creator Noonian Soong who claims to be his "mother".
writer: Dan Koeppel, René Echevarria, Naren Shankar

title: Initiations
description: Chakotay is captured by a young Kazon who is undergoing a manhood ritual.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Lisa Klink

title: Innocence
description: Tuvok crashes on a planet where he finds some children who know that they are all going to die.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink, Anthony Williams

title: Inquisition
description: Dr. Bashir's plans to attend a medical conference at a breezy resort are canceled when Starfleet Intelligence boards DS9, suspecting the doctor of treason.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bradley Thompson, David Weddle

title: Inside Man
description: Direct from Starfleet a hologram of Barclay arrives with instructions to get Voyager home within three days. The Doctor suspects that there is something wrong with the hologram.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Doherty, Michael Taylor

title: Inter Arma Enim Silent Leges
description: While making plans to attend a medical conference on the Romulan home world, Dr. Bashir is contacted by Sloan of Section 31 and recruited to determine the health of a high-ranking Romulan official. Things turn more desperate, though, when the ranking Starfleet admiral traveling with him, who knows about Section 31's interest in the conference mysteriously suffers a brain aneurysm after Dr. Bashir finds out that Sloan is traveling with the group, as well.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, David Weddle

title: Interface
description: Geordi is distracted by the news of the possible loss of his mother while he uses a new remote probe technology to search for a downed spacecraft.
writer: Joe Menosky, René Echevarria, Naren Shankar

title: Invasive Procedures
description: A jealous Trill, who had waited his entire life to receive a symbiont but never did, decides to steal Dax's.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, John Whelpley, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Investigations
description: Tom Paris leaves Voyager and joins a Talaxian space convoy.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Jeff Schnaufer, Ed Bond

title: Is There in Truth No Beauty?
description: Both humans and Klingons have been lured to a planet by a formless entity that feeds on hatred and has set about to fashion them into a permanent food supply for itself.
writer: Jerome Bixby, Arthur H. Singer

title: It's Only a Paper Moon
description: Severely depressed at his serious war wound, Nog retreats into Vic Fontaine's holosuite program while the singer tries to help him.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, David Mack

title: Jetrel
description: As Neelix's cooking sickens Voyager itself, Tuvok runs the four most problematic Maquis malcontents through a Starfleet-style boot camp.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald Wilkerson, Jean Louise Matthias

title: Journey to Babel
description: To protect a space station with a vital grain shipment, Kirk and the crew must deal with Federation bureaucrats, a Klingon battle cruiser and a peddler who sells furry, purring, voracious little creatures as pets.
writer: David Gerrold

title: Journey's End
description: After the Federation grants access by the Cardassians to a planet already inhabited by Native Americans, Picard has the daunting task of relocating them.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, Shawn Piller, Antonia King

title: Judgment
description: After Enterprise lends aid to a group of accused rebels, Captain Archer faces a tribunal and charges of conspiring against the Klingon Empire.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, David A. Goodman, Taylor Elmore

title: Juggernaut
description: As The Doctor guides Seven into the unfamiliar social realm of dating, Ambassador Neelix suffers an alien monk who overindulges.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Taylor, Bryan Fuller

title: Justice
description: Beware Ferengi bearing gifts. Capt. Picard receives the Stargazer, a ship considered lost that he once commanded.
writer: Herbert Wright, Larry Forrester

title: Kayshon, His Eyes Open
description: Our Lower Deckers have trouble bonding with Ensign Jet Manhaver, who has been assigned Boimler's bunk and shift duties. Meanwhile, we get a glimpse of Boimler's life on the U.S.S. Titan, which is more intense than he thought it would be.

title: Kir'Shara
description: While Enterprise travels to Andoria to warn them of the attack, Archer tries to bring the Kir'Shara to the High Command.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman

title: Kobayashi
description: As Gwyn struggles to find her role aboard the USS Protostar, Dal tests his leadership skills in the newly discovered holodeck.

title: Latent Image
description: The Doctor discovers a profoundly personal mystery when evidence of performing surgery on Ens. Kim eighteen months ago arises - for which the Doctor has no memory.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Eileen Connors

title: Legacy
description: The crew of the Enterprise enters into orbit around the home planet of their former crewmate, Tasha Yar, where they encounter her sister.
writer: Joe Menosky, Ronald D. Moore

title: Lessons
description: Picard falls for the new head of the stellar science services department, but has feelings of misgivings when he's forced to assign her to a dangerous mission.
writer: Ronald Wilkerson, Jean Louise Matthias, Brannon Braga

title: Let He Who Is Without Sin...
description: While vacationing on Risa, Worf and Dax meet a group of fundamentalist protesters who claim that the Federation has gone soft.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe, David Weddle

title: Let Sleeping Borg Lie
description: When the crew encounters a dormant Borg Cube, Zero risks everything to save their ship.

title: Let That Be Your Last Battlefield
description: The Enterprise encounters two duo-chromatic and mutually belligerent aliens who put the ship in the middle of their old conflict.
writer: Oliver Crawford, Gene L. Coon, Arthur H. Singer

title: Liaisons
