description: On a diplomatic mission, Worf, Troi and the Captain deal with three aliens who explore foreign concepts with extreme, singular focus.
writer: Jeanne Carrigan-Fauci, Lisa Rich, Roger Eschbacher

title: Life Line
description: The Doctor gets himself transmitted back to Federation space to treat his mortally ailing creator, Dr. Lewis Zimmerman. A clash of egos ensues.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Doherty, Raf Green

title: Life Support
description: Dr. Bashir must use extraordinary measures to prolong Vedek Bareil's life long enough to allow Bareil to complete sensitive peace negotiations between the Bajorans and the Cardassians.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, Christian Ford

title: Lifesigns
description: The Doctor saves a Vidiian dying from the Phage by placing her consciousness in a holographic body, and then begins to fall in love with her.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Lisa Klink

title: Lift Us Where Suffering Cannot Reach
description: A threat to an idyllic planet reunites Captain Pike with the lost love of his life. To protect her and a scientific holy child from a conspiracy, Pike offers his help and is forced to face unresolved feelings of his past.

title: Lineage
description: Voyager rescues a prison warden and a set of prisoners that are scheduled to be executed testing their own ethical beliefs.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Doherty, Michael Sussman

title: Little Green Men
description: Worf, Dax and the Klingon Dahar master Kor set out to find an ancient lost relic: the Sword of Kahless.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, Richard Danus

title: Live Fast and Prosper
description: A trio of impostors give Voyager, Janeway, Tuvok, Chakotay and the Federation a bad name.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robin Bernheim, Robert Doherty

title: Living Witness
description: The Doctor awakens in the museum of an alien culture seven hundred years in the future, where Voyager is thought to have been a passing warship full of cold-blooded killers.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bryan Fuller, Brannon Braga

title: Lonely Among Us
description: On an alien planet, young Wesley Crusher commits a transgression - small by our standards but mandatorily punishable by death in theirs.
writer: Worley Thorne, John D.F. Black

title: Looking for par'Mach in All the Wrong Places
description: While Worf helps Quark to pursue his former wife Grilka, O'Brien and Kira grow uncomfortably close to each other.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore

title: Lost and Found
description: The young alien Dal finds a hidden starfleet spaceship during an escape attempt from a mine planet. But who is still after the ship?

title: Loud As A Whisper
description: The crew ferries a deaf mediator to Solais V to negotiate an end to a civil war.
writer: Jacqueline Zambrano, Leonard Mlodinow, Scott Rubenstein

title: Lower Decks
description: A mission of the Enterprise is seen through the eyes of some junior officers who are up for promotions, as questions of duty and honor arise among some of them, such as a Bajoran whom Worf has designated to be promoted to operations.
writer: René Echevarria, Ronald Wilkerson, Jean Louise Matthias

title: Macrocosm
description: After returning from a first contact mission, Janeway, Neelix and the Doctor must retake Voyager from an infestation of microorganisms that grow to an alarming size.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Lisa Klink

title: Magic to Make the Sanest Man Go Mad
Man Of The People
description: When the Enterprise comes to the rescue of an ambassador and his "mother", Deanna finds herself attracted to him, and seeks to comfort him upon her death.
writer: Frank Abatemarco, Brannon Braga, René Echevarria

title: Maneuvers
description: After the Kazon steal some Federation technology Chakotay goes after them on his own and is captured.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Lisa Klink

title: Manhunt
description: A midlife Betazoid condition renders Ambassador Troi hot on the hunt for romance and wedlock with Captain Picard - or any man.
writer: Tracy Tormé, Melinda M. Snodgrass

title: Maps and Legends
description: Without the support of Starfleet, Picard turns to Dr. Agnes Jurati and his estranged colleague Raffi Musiker for help in finding the truth about Dahj, unaware that hidden enemies are also interested in what he'll find.

title: Marauders
description: Enterprise visits a colony with a deuterium trading post. But the people seem to be pretty unwilling to trade. Are they hiding something?
writer: Gene Roddenberry, David Wilcox, Rick Berman

title: Masks
description: Investigating a rogue comet that's been traveling throughout the universe for over 87 million years, Picard discovers alien beings within the comet, which infest the computer systems on the Enterprise, as well as Data.
writer: Joe Menosky, René Echevarria, Naren Shankar

title: Masquerade
description: Trapped in the Neutral Zone, the crew encounters a rogue geneticist who sheds light on Dal's past.

title: Meld
description: Tuvok mind-melds with a murderous Maquis crewman to better understand the roots of serial killing, losing control of himself in the process.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman, Kenneth Biller

title: Melora
description: Melora Pazlar, a new arrival on DS9 with a special gravity-based disability, finds an admirer in Bashir. Meanwhile, an old associate of Quark's comes to the station to kill the bartender.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Evan Somers, Steven Baum

title: Memento Mori
description: Pike must find unconventional Starfleet methods to deal with a malevolent force that attacks the Enterprise.

title: Memorial
description: Members of an away mission start dreaming about a species they have never seen before who are in a gruesome battle on an unknown planet.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robin Bernheim, Robert Doherty

title: Mercy
description: With time running out before the launch of the Europa Mission, Picard and Guinan must free themselves from FBI custody. Seven and Raffi come face-to-face with Jurati and the horror of what she's become.

title: Meridian
description: Dax falls in love with one of the residents of a multi-dimensional planet, while on DS9 an alien requests a holosuite program of Kira from Quark.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Mark O'Connell, Hilary Bader

title: Message in a Bottle
description: Using an alien communications net, Voyager sends their Doctor to the Federation ship USS Prometheus only to find that it has been taken over by Romulans.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink, Rick Williams

title: Metamorphosis
description: A powerful being claiming to be the Greek god Apollo appears and demands that the crew of the Enterprise disembark onto his planet to worship him.
writer: Gilbert Ralston

title: Mindwalk
description: Desperate to warn Starfleet of their dilemma, a daring experiment goes awry as Dal inadvertently swaps mind with a Starfleet Vice Admiral.

title: Minefield
description: The safety of Enterprise rests on Reed's shoulders when the ship becomes trapped in a Romulan minefield and Romulan ships are closing in on them.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, John Shiban, André Bormanis

title: Mining The Mind's Mines
description: On a remote science outpost, stone orbs are bringing fantasies to life. Tendi starts her first day as a Senior Science Officer Trainee.

title: Miri
description: Spock kidnaps the disabled Capt. Pike, hijacks the Enterprise, and then surrenders for court martial.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, John D.F. Black

title: Mirror, Mirror
description: The Enterprise hosts a number of quarrelling diplomats, including Spock's father, but someone on board has murder in mind.
writer: D.C. Fontana

title: Moist Vessel
description: Captain Freeman seeks the ultimate payback after Mariner blatantly disrespects her in front of the crew. A well-meaning Tendi accidentally messes up a lieutenant's attempt at spiritual ascension and tries to make it right.

title: Monsters
description: Tallinn enters Picard's subconscious mind to help him wake from a coma and face his deepest fears; Seven and Raffi search for Jurati fearing she's succumbed to her inner monster; Rios struggles to hide the truth about himself from Teresa.

title: More Tribbles, More Troubles
description: Cyrano Jones returns, infesting the Enterprise with tribbles that now grow to monstrous size.

title: Mortal Coil
description: Thanks to Seven of Nine, Neelix returns from the dead, but his lack of a glimpse of the afterlife brings about a severe crisis of faith.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bryan Fuller, Lisa Klink

title: Move Along Home
description: The Wadi from the Gamma Quadrant visit DS9, thereby making first contact with the Federation. When they catch Quark cheating, they let him off by playing a special game.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Frederick Rappaport, Lisa Rich

title: Much Ado About Boimler
description: Mariner tries to impress her best friend from Starfleet Academy who is now a visiting captain. Boimler is sent to a Starfleet medical ship after a transporter accident puts him "out of phase."

title: Mudd's Passion
description: The Enterprise crew once again runs afoul of the incorrigible Harry Mudd, this time hawking a love potion that greatly influences Spock and the crew.

title: Mudd's Women
description: The flight recorder of the 200-year-old U.S.S. Valiant relays a tale of terror--a magnetic storm at the edge of the galaxy.
writer: Samuel A. Peeples

title: Mugato, Gumato
description: The USS Cerritos is dispatched to a planet to investigate an unexplained sighting of a dangerous Mugato.

title: Muse
description: An alien playwright discovers B'Elanna Torres, crash landed in the Delta Flier, and uses her and her logs as story material to please his patron.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Robert Doherty

title: Ménage à Troi
description: When Deanna's mother spurns advances made by a Ferengi Daimon, he takes it upon himself to kidnap the two of them, along with Commander Riker and steal them away aboard his ship.
writer: Fred Bronson, Susan Sackett, Ronald D. Moore

title: Natural Law
description: Voyager finds a colony of Talaxians far from their home planet. When Neelix begins to bond with them, he makes a major decision.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Raf Green, Michael Taylor

title: Necessary Evil
description: Odo relives some terrible and tragic memories of a past investigation when he was the constable on Cardassian-occupied Deep Space Nine, then named Terok Nor.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Peter Allan Fields, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Nemesis
description: Shot down by crossfire, Chakotay bonds with one faction in the midst of a war.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Lisa Klink

title: Nepenthe
description: Picard and Soji, who's struggling to make sense of her recently unlocked memories, travel to a planet that happens to be the home of Picard's old friends Will Riker and Deanna Troi; Elnor and Hugh are left on the Borg cube to face Narissa.

title: New Ground
description: Geordi can barely curtail his enthusiasm at being able to test out a new theory of transportation, as Worf's mother boards the Enterprise with his son and news that Worf's Earthly parents can no longer care for him.
writer: Grant Rosenberg, Sara B Cooper, Stuart Charno

title: Night
description: Passing through a vast starless void, monotony hits the crew hard until a darkness species attacks, mistaking Voyager as an ally of a poisonous garbage freighter.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Bryan Fuller

title: Night Terrors
description: The Enterprise gets trapped in a Tyken's Rift, an energy-absorbing rupture in space, where all but Data, Troi and Guinan experience rising hallucinations and paranoia.
writer: Pamela Douglas, Jeri Taylor, Shari Goodhartz

title: Nightingale
description: When the Delta Flyer comes to the aid of a medical transport, Harry Kim gets his first command.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, André Bormanis, Robert Lederman

title: No Small Parts
description: The USS Cerritos encounters a familiar enemy. Tendi helps a struggling recruit find her footing.

title: No Win Scenario
description: Picard, Riker and crew must confront the sins of their past as the Titan drifts helplessly in a mysterious space anomaly.

title: Non Sequitur
description: Harry Kim wakes up in San Francisco having never been assigned to Voyager which Starfleet considers lost.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Kenneth Biller

title: Nor the Battle to the Strong
description: Jake, reporting from the front lines, sees what war truly is.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, Brice Parker

title: North Star
description: Enterprise investigates a previously unknown colony of humans who are living in the fashion of the American Old West. They are oppressing an alien race called the Skagarans.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, David A. Goodman, André Bormanis

title: Nothing Human
description: When an alien parasitically latches onto B'Elanna for survival, the Doctor calls upon a holographic Cardassian doctor for assistance, unaware he's a war criminal, thereby creating an ethical quandary.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Jeri Taylor, Bryan Fuller

title: Oasis
description: The crew of Enterprise searches for a seemingly abandoned ship on a desert planet.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Stephen Beck, Rick Berman

title: Observer Effect
description: Noncorporeal aliens inhabit the bodies of Enterprise crew members to study how the crew reacts to a lethal virus infection that has afflicted Trip and Hoshi.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Garfield Reeves-Stevens, Judith Reeves-Stevens

title: Obsession
description: The Enterprise encounters a gigantic energy draining space organism that threatens the galaxy.
writer: Robert Sabaroff

title: Once More Unto the Breach
description: An aged Kor requests an opportunity to die with honor as a warrior.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, David Weddle

title: Once Upon a Planet
description: Upon returning to the amusement park planet, the Enterprise crew finds the computer, which generates the planet's sophisticated robots, running amok, with the caretaker nowhere to be found.

title: Once Upon a Time
description: Long traumatized by the loss of his own family, Neelix balks at telling Naomi that her mother and others have gone missing on an away mission.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Taylor, Bryan Fuller

title: One
description: Travel through a toxic nebula puts nearly all the Voyager crew in stasis, restricts the Doctor to sickbay, degrades the ship's systems and leaves Seven solely in charge.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink, Bryan Fuller

title: One Little Ship
description: A Dominion attack on the Defiant endangers Dax, Bashir and O'Brien aboard the runabout U.S.S. Rubicon as they enter an anomaly that shrinks them to a very minute size. Unfortunately, they were being held stable by the Defiant within a tractor beam, which was discontinued during the attack.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, David Weddle, Bradley Thompson

title: One Small Step
description: Voyager crosses paths with a rare spatial anomaly that swallowed an Earth ship orbiting Mars in 2032 (a discovery that calls for an away mission).
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Mike Wollaeger, Jessica Scott

title: One of Our Planets Is Missing
description: The crew of the Enterprise must stop a living planet-consuming cloud before it attacks an inhabited world.

title: Operation -- Annihilate!
description: The Enterprise crew attempts to stop a plague of amoeba-like creatures from possessing human hosts and spreading throughout the galaxy.
writer: Steven W. Carabatsos

title: Our Man Bashir
description: Sisko travels to Earth when a bombing at a Federation conference is determined to be the work of Changelings.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Paradise
description: Sisko and Chief O'Brien discover a colony which lives without technology.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Jeff King, Richard Manning

title: Paradise Lost
description: First Minister Shakaar visits the station, despite a threat of assassination. He falls in love with Kira much to Odo's dismay.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria

title: Parallax
description: The Voyager crew discovers a planet which recently suffered a horrific catastrophe. Upon investigation, Janeway and Paris are sent back in time before the disaster and are faced with the decision of whether to try to stop it.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, David Kemper, Kenneth Biller

title: Parallels
description: On his return from a bat'leth competition in the Klingon Empire, Worf finds himself shifting realities where events and details are in a constant state of flux and only he is aware of the changes.
writer: Brannon Braga, René Echevarria, Naren Shankar

title: Parturition
description: After a disagreement over Kes, Neelix and Tom Paris are trapped together on an alien planet and must work together so that they and their alien baby ward can survive.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Tom Szollosi, Kenneth Biller

title: Past Prologue
description: Tahna Los, a former Bajoran terrorist during the Occupation, asks Sisko for asylum on DS9. Meanwhile, the station's last Cardassian inhabitant, Garak, possibly a former spy for the Cardassian government, proves an interesting mystery to Dr. Bashir.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Katharyn Powers, Peter Allan Fields

title: Past Tense, Part I
description: Sisko, Bashir and Dax are accidentally sent to San Francisco in the 21st century due to a transporter malfunction, and must figure out how to get back to return without changing the time line.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Past Tense, Part II
description: Sisko is forced to take the place of a key historical figure on Earth in 2024 in order to preserve the timeline.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Pathfinder
description: On Earth, Barclay uses holograms to formulate a plan to open communications with Voyager.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, David Zabel, Kenneth Biller

title: Patterns of Force
description: Responding to a distress signal, Kirk finds Captain Tracey of the U.S.S. Exeter violating the prime directive and interfering with a war between the Yangs and the Kohms to find the secret of their longevity.
writer: Gene Roddenberry

title: Peak Performance
description: The crew of the Enterprise participates in war games.
writer: David Kemper, Melinda M. Snodgrass

title: Pen Pals
description: Data befriends an alien girl in distress, breaking the Prime Directive, while Wesley commands his first team.
writer: Melinda M. Snodgrass, Hannah Louise Shearer

title: Penance
description: Picard finds himself transported to an alternate timeline in the year 2400 where he must face one last trial orchestrated by his longtime nemesis Q.

title: Penumbra
description: When the ship Worf was serving on is attacked in battle and all search efforts are abandoned, Ezri takes a runabout in search of him against Sisko's orders. Meanwhile, Sisko proposes to Kasidy Yates.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, David Weddle

title: Persistence of Vision
description: The Voyager crew enters a new region of space and begins to see hallucinations. The Captain sees her holonovel characters come to life.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Lisa Klink

title: Phage
description: Voyager becomes trapped in a strange nebula when the crew searches for a new power source for the ship.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Tom Szollosi, Brannon Braga

title: Phantasms
description: Data deals with his new ability to experience nightmares as the Enterprise deals with the installation of a new warp core that won't work as expected.
writer: Brannon Braga, René Echevarria, Naren Shankar

title: Plato's Stepchildren
description: Trapped in an alien laboratory, Kirk, Spock and McCoy meet an empath and are involved in a series of experiments.
writer: Joyce Muskat, Arthur H. Singer

title: Playing God
description: A Trill named Arjin comes to DS9 to learn what it is to be a Trill with a symbiont but finds his teacher, Dax, to be less than he expected.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Jim Trombetta, Michael Piller

title: Power Play
description: A long-lost ship is found on a planet where cosmic storms prevented detection for 200 years. When a surface landing goes wrong, the away team is beamed back...with a few extra surprises.
writer: Rene Balcer, Herbert Wright, Brannon Braga

title: Precious Cargo
description: While Trip is repairing a stasis pod at a Retellian cargo ship and the woman inside, heir apparent to the throne of Krios, wakes up, the ship makes a hasty departure. Trip must make a plan to escape.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, David A. Goodman, Rick Berman

title: Preemptive Strike
description: Following her return from Advanced Tactical Training, Bajoran Ro Laren is sent to infiltrate the Maquis and finds herself torn between her loyalty to Picard and Starfleet, and her sympathy with the Maquis fighting "the good fight".
writer: René Echevarria, Naren Shankar

title: Preludes
description: A Starfleet admiral digs into the past of the Protostar crew. Meanwhile, the Diviner recalls his life's mission.

title: Prey
description: Helping a wounded Hirogen, Janeway finds that their prey has boarded Voyager, a member of Species 8472.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Lisa Klink

title: Prime Factors
description: A fatal disaster aboard a Kazon ship reveals mishandled Federation technology at its heart, which indicates a traitor aboard Voyager.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Chris Abbott, Paul Robert Coyle

title: Prodigal Daughter
description: Ezri travels to her family home in search of Chief O'Brien who has discovered a murdered woman connected with the family and the Orion Syndicate.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, David Weddle, Bradley Thompson

title: Profit and Lace
description: Quark's mother and the Grand Nagus show up on DS9, announcing a new amendment to the Bill of Opportunities he has instituted on the home planet of Ferenginar, and then follows up with the announcement, that, due to the new amendment, chaos has followed and he has been deposed.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, David Weddle

title: Profit and Loss
description: A Cardassian woman named Natima Lang, who was once a flame of Quark's, visits the station under dubious circumstances.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Flip Kobler, Cindy Marcus

title: Progress
description: Kira must convince an old Bajoran farmer to leave a moon becoming uninhabitable due to mining operations. Jake and Nog try to trade off Cardassian yamok sauce.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Peter Allan Fields, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Projections
description: The Doctor tries to determine what is real and what is not when he is activated due to what appears to be a ship-wide emergency.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Kenneth Biller

title: Prophecy
description: Voyager slips into a pocket of subspace where many other ships are trapped and must steal from each other to survive. Only with the help of other star ships can they all escape the void.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Raf Green, James Kahn

title: Prophet Motive
description: Grand Nagus Zek is acting bizarre by giving away money and rewriting the Rules of Acquisition. Quark attempts to get the old Nagus back.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe, William N. Stape

title: Prototype
description: B'Elanna gives aid to a failing automated device found adrift in space. After the repairs, the device recalls what its purpose was - and carries it out.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Nicholas Corea, Kenneth Biller

title: Proving Ground
description: Enterprise has found the location of the testing site of the Xindi weapon and gets some rather unexpected help.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Chris Black, André Bormanis

title: Q Who
description: Q tries to prove that Picard needs him as part of their crew by hurling the Enterprise 7,000 light years away where they encounter the Borg for the first time.
writer: Melinda M. Snodgrass

title: Q-Less
description: Q, the Enterprise-D's consistent omnipotent annoyance, comes to harass the DS9 crew when his traveling companion, Vash, refuses to travel with Q any longer.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe, Hannah Louise Shearer

title: Q2
description: The Doctor writes and publishes an incendiary holonovel that defames the crew, Paris' tampering offends the author, and The Doctor's rights come into question.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Phyllis Strong, Michael Sussman

title: Qpid
description: Aiming to get out of Jean-Luc's debt, Q forces Capt. Picard and his officers to live out the Legend of Robin Hood - with a past acquaintance as Maid Marian.
writer: Ira Steven Behr, Randee Russell, Ronald D. Moore

title: Rajiin
description: Enterprise helps an alien woman, Rajiin, seeking to escape her life as a sex slave. Once on board she uses her alien abilities to gather biometric data about humans and is recaptured by her employers, the Xindi council.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brent Friedman, Chris Black

title: Random Thoughts
description: A telepathic race arrests B'Elanna for thinking violent thoughts that have begun to infect their peacefully enlightened, virtually crime-free society.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Lisa Klink

title: Rapture
description: Sisko begins having visions that may show him how to best help Bajor, but they're killing him.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, L.J. Strom

title: Rascals
description: An emergency beam out renders four crewmen down to childhood shortly before renegade Ferengi take over the Enterprise and offload the adults.
writer: Allison Hock, Ward Botsford, Diana Dru Botsford

title: Real Life
description: As Captain Janeway leads an investigation into an unheard of space phenomenon, B'Elanna tweaks the Doctor's all too sugary creation of a holographic family towards "real life."
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Harry 'Doc' Kloor, Lisa Klink

title: Realm Of Fear
description: Overcoming his fear of transporter, Lt. Barclay joins an away team, only to find something in the beam with him.
writer: Brannon Braga, René Echevarria, Naren Shankar

title: Redemption
description: Both Captain Picard and Lt. Worf must decide where their priorities lie as the Klingon Empire descends into a bitter civil war.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, Joe Menosky, David Carren

title: Redemption II
description: As Worf fights in the Klingon Civil War, Captain Picard and his crew must stop the Romulans from helping their pawns achieve victory.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, Brannon Braga, René Echevarria

title: Reflections
description: Mariner and Boimler work the Starfleet recruitment booth at an alien job fair, Rutherford challenges himself.

title: Regeneration
description: An Arctic research team makes a startling find: cybernetic creatures - Borg - buried in the ice. When the revived aliens seize control of a spacecraft, it's up to Archer to keep them from contacting the Collective and threatening Earth.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman, Phyllis Strong

title: Rejoined
description: Quark and Rom take Nog to Earth and Starfleet Academy, but a malfunction with the ship takes the crew back in time, to Roswell, New Mexico in 1947.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe, Toni Marberry

title: Relativity
description: An alien weapon that possesses artificial intelligence links with the EMH program and begins to terrorize the crew.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Taylor, Kenneth Biller

title: Relics
description: The Enterprise stumbles upon a Dyson sphere, with a ship crashed on the outer surface. An away team finds some systems still powered up and the chief engineer from the old Enterprise, Montgomery Scott, locked in the transporter cycle.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, Brannon Braga, René Echevarria

title: Remember
description: Lieutenant Torres starts having vivid dreams about another life, another love, and another planet.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink, Brannon Braga

title: Remember Me
description: Dr. Crusher's fear of losing loved ones becomes real when her worries create an alternate reality.
writer: Lee Sheldon, Ronald D. Moore, Joe Menosky

title: Remembrance
description: Fourteen years after retiring from Starfleet, Jean-Luc Picard, still haunted by the death of Data, is living a quiet life on his family vineyard when a woman comes to him for help.

title: Renaissance Man
description: Having long since made it home, an aged Admiral Janeway breaks Starfleet directives and temporal laws to take a last stab at an old enemy and shorten Voyager's journey home.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Doherty, Rick Berman

title: Repentance
description: Voyager finds a multi-generational Klingon ship that left the Alpha Quadrant more than 100 years before. When they hear of B'Elanna's child, they claim it as their savior.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman, Phyllis Strong

title: Repression
description: Security officer Tuvok investigates a strange series of attacks on Voyager directed at the former Maquis members.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Mark Haskell Smith, Michael Taylor

title: Requiem for Methuselah
description: Kirk and Spock are caught up in a revolution on a planet where intellectuals and artists live on a utopian city in the sky while the rest of the population toils in mines on the barren surface below.
writer: Margaret Armen, David Gerrold, Oliver Crawford

title: Resistance
description: Disguised while on an away mission Captain Janeway is rescued by a man who thinks that she is his daughter.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink, Michael Jan Friedman

title: Resolutions
description: Captain Janeway and Chakotay contract an incurable virus and they can only survive if they stay behind together on a planet which negates the effects of the disease.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink

title: Resurrection
description: Major Kira takes a romantic interest in the mirror universe counterpart of her deceased lover Bareil after he transports himself into her universe and onto DS9.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Taylor, David Weddle

title: Retrospect
description: Seven of Nine claims to have been assaulted by the arms trader who just sold new technology to Voyager.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bryan Fuller, Lisa Klink

title: Return to Grace
description: Worf's brother Kurn asks him to perform a death rite to regain his lost honor. Meanwhile, Kira and O'Brien investigate Klingon activity near the Bajoran border.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore

title: Return to Tomorrow
description: Galactic alien scouts hijack the Enterprise for a return voyage to their home galaxy and a prelude to invasion. Kirk's only advantage is that they're not fully adapted to the human form they've taken on.
writer: D.C. Fontana, Jerome Bixby

title: Reunion
description: When the leader of the Klingon High Council dies, Picard finds himself in the middle of the struggle for the now-vacant position. Meanwhile, Worf reunites with a past love, only to find he now has a son.
writer: Thomas Perry, Jo Perry, Ronald D. Moore

title: Revulsion
description: Amidst promotions, added duties and shifts in relationships, Voyager responds to a distress call from a hologram, one who proves himself dangerously unsafe to be around.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink, Bryan Fuller

title: Riddles
description: Sudden contact with a cloaked and extremely xenophobic race leaves Tuvok brain damaged. Neelix puts himself in charge of his rehabilitation.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Doherty, André Bormanis

title: Rightful Heir
description: During Worf's crisis of faith on Boreth, Kahless the Unforgettable makes his promised legendary return, but Klingon leader Gowron challenges his authenticity.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, James E. Brooks, Brannon Braga

title: Rise
description: While helping a planet being bombarded by asteroids, Neelix and Tuvok get trapped on the surface, where Tuvok's negative attitude gets the better of Neelix's nerves.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Jimmy Diggs

title: Rivals
description: Quark meets his match when a new rival opens a casino opposite his bar on the Promenade. Meanwhile, the station is suffering issues of high improbability.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Jim Trombetta

title: Rocks and Shoals
description: Sisko and his crew crash on a barren world when their commandeered Jem'Hadar ship is shot down. They encounter Jem'Hadar who crashed there earlier, and have taken Nog and Garak hostage in exchange for medical aid for their Vorta overseer.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, David Weddle

title: Rogue Planet
description: Lieutenant Reed discovers a rogue planet, which has broken out of orbit and therefore is devoid of any light. Life is still possible in some places because heat is bubbling up from under the surface, but T'Pol doesn't detect any humanoid life. Further scans however give a strange energy reading which seems to be coming from a ship on the surface. After the crew has landed, they encounter the Eska. The Eska come from another planet and they say they are hunting for the drayjin, an animal considered a delicacy. When captain Jonathan Archer tries to sleep on the planet, he hears a voice calling him by his name. A human female is wandering in the forest. Somehow he seems to know her.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Chris Black, Rick Berman

title: Room for Growth
description: Mariner, Boimler, and Tendi clash with their arch-rivals: Delta Shift. The Cerritos engineers go on mandatory relaxation leave.

title: Rules of Acquisition
description: A female Ferengi named Pel, posing as a male so that she can engage in business, falls for Quark during very important negotiations between the Ferengi and a Gamma Quadrant race.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ira Steven Behr, Hilary Bader

title: Rules of Engagement
description: O'Brien tries to re-integrate to life on the station after serving 20 years in a virtual prison.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe, Daniel Keys Moran

title: Sacred Ground
description: After Kes is injured by an energy field on a planet's sacred ground, Janeway must undergo a spiritual quest in order to save her life.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink, Geo Athena Trevarthen

title: Sacrifice of Angels
description: The Federation attacks DS9 in hopes of keeping the mine field at the wormhole intact, while the Dominion waits entrance to the Alpha Quadrant from the other side.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, David Weddle

title: Samaritan Snare
description: With Picard away for routine surgery, the Enterprise responds to an apparent distress call from a Pakled vessel.
writer: Robert McCullough, Melinda M. Snodgrass

title: Sanctuary
description: A race that has been conquered twice over, one of the conquerors being the mysterious Dominion, comes to the station seeking aid and a new home.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Frederick Rappaport, Gabe Essoe

title: Sarek
description: Legendary Federation ambassador Sarek visits the Enterprise to conclude peace talks with a race called the Legarans. His arrival is accompanied with a rash of unusual emotional outbursts among the crew.
writer: Peter S. Beagle, Marc Cushman, Jake Jacobs

title: Schisms
description: Commander Riker's inability to fall asleep begins to show in other members of the crew in other forms, putting the Enterprise on the trail of a mystery.
writer: Brannon Braga, Jean Louise Matthias, Ronald Wilkerson

title: Scientific Method
description: A string of bizarre illnesses afflicts the Voyager crew. The Doctor and Seven of Nine uncover a team of alien researchers existing out of phase performing medical experiments on the ship's crew.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink, Sherry Klein

title: Scorpion
description: About to enter Borg space, Voyager finds a threat so devastating that even the Borg cannot deal with it.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Joe Menosky

title: Scorpion, Part II
description: Voyager finds a solution to combat the invader of Borg space. All Captain Janeway asks is free passage through their territory and Voyager will share their knowledge.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Joe Menosky

title: Second Chances
description: Riker's exact duplicate is discovered on Nervala IV. His first order of business: resume his relationship with Deanna. Second order: obey orders from his superior - himself.
writer: René Echevarria, Michael A. Medlock, Brannon Braga

title: Second Contact
description: Ensigns Mariner and Boimler run into difficulty on Galar. Meanwhile, an alien virus infects the crew of the Cerritos.

title: Second Sight
description: While noted scientist Gideon Seyetik is preparing for an ambitious project, re-igniting a star, Sisko is intrigued by a woman who keeps disappearing.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Mark O'Connell, Ira Steven Behr

title: Second Skin
description: Kira finds herself on Cardassia as a Cardassian. She is told she is called Iliana Ghemor and was a Cardassian spy.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Seventeen Seconds
description: Picard grapples with a life-altering revelation as the crew of the Titan attempt to outmaneuver Vadic, while Raffi and Worf uncover a plot by a vengeful enemy.

title: Shades of Gray
description: When Commander Riker comes down with a dangerous alien infection after an away mission, the only way to treat it may be through reliving his memories.
writer: Richard Manning, Hans Beimler, Melinda M. Snodgrass

title: Shadowplay
description: Odo and Dax investigate the mysterious disappearance of people from a village. Kira tries to keep a close eye on Quark, while Vedek Bareil unexpectedly visits.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Shadows and Symbols
description: Ezri Dax joins Sisko and his family on his quest for the Orb of the Emissary. Colonel Kira mounts a blockade around a Bajoran moon, where the Romulan hospital is - along with thousands of Romulan missles (claimed to be defensive in purpose). Quark joins Bashir & O'Brien as they join Worf, aboard a Klingon warship as they mount a dangerous mission in Jadzia Dax's name in an effort to get her spirit into Sto-vo-kor, the Klingon's heaven.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, David Weddle

title: Shadows of P'Jem
description: T'Pol is being recalled because of Enterprise's involvement in the destruction of the P'Jem sanctuary. She and Captain Archer are later kidnapped while visiting the planet Coridan.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman, Phyllis Strong

title: Shakaar
description: News arrives that Kai Winn has also become Bajor's political leader. She asks Kira to negotiate with an old resistance friend about returning soil reclamators.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Gordon T. Dawson, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Shattered
description: Tom and B'Elanna are overjoyed about their coming child; however, B'Elanna soon starts to have unpleasant flashbacks about her difficult childhood.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, James Kahn, Michael Taylor

title: Shattered Mirror
description: A mysterious woman approaches Jake about his future as a writer. Odo promises to do whatever it takes to help Lwaxana Troi keep her baby over the father's wishes - even if it means marrying her himself.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, Majel Barrett

title: Ship In A Bottle
description: Lt. Barclay mistakenly awakes Moriarty in the forgotten holodeck program, who then makes his demands clear and unforgettable.
writer: René Echevarria, Brannon Braga, Arthur Conan Doyle

title: Shockwave
description: Enterprise discovers a mining colony of the Paraagans. The crew gets an invitation for a meeting on the surface and a shuttle pod tries to break through the atmosphere. All of a sudden just under the shuttle a giant explosion begins and scorches the earth beneath. All 3600 colonists are reported dead. After examination, Enterprise finds out plasma leaking from the pod's vents ignited tetrazine in the atmosphere. Lieutenant Reed, however, ensures he closed the plasma vents before the shuttle went of. After conversations with admiral Forrest of Starfleet Command, Captain Archer learns that Enterprise's mission is canceled and they must return home, not to return to space for another ten or twenty years. When the ship has altered course, crewman Daniels, a time traveler, pays a visit to captain Archer.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Rick Berman, Phyllis Strong

title: Shockwave, Part II
description: Captain Archer and temporal agent Daniels have been stranded in the 31st century. In a library they investigate the damage to the time line and try to find way a to get Archer back in his own century. Meanwhile Silik the Suliban leader is trying his utmost to find the whereabouts of Archer and he is willing to go far for the answer. But the crew of Enterprise has plans of their own. Trip finds a way to use the com system and is able to speak with Reed. Together they try to contact other crew members and make plans to purge Enterprise of the Sulibans.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Rick Berman, André Bormanis

title: Shore Leave
description: A being that controls matter and creates planets wants to play with the Enterprise crew.
writer: Paul Schneider

title: Shuttlepod One
description: While returning to Enterprise in Shuttlepod One, Trip and Reed see apparent evidence that the ship has been destroyed. With only days of oxygen left, they must find a way to survive long enough for rescue.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Rick Berman, Phyllis Strong

title: Silent Enemy
description: After an attack by a mysterious ship, Archers orders Enterprise's return to Jupiter to install phase cannons; Hoshi makes plans for Malcolm's birthday.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, André Bormanis, Phyllis Strong

title: Silicon Avatar
description: The Crystalline Entity returns, and the Enterprise takes aboard a scientist to help track it down, but her personal agenda does not match Capt. Picard's.
writer: Jeri Taylor, Lawrence V. Conley, Brannon Braga

title: Similitude
description: Archer decides to make a fast-growing clone of Trip to help save his life while Enterprise is stranded in a dangerous field with nucleonic particles.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Manny Coto, André Bormanis

title: Singularity
description: T'Pol must save the ship after the crew becomes obsessed with trivialities during the approach to a black hole.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Chris Black, André Bormanis

title: Sins of The Father
description: A Klingon commander comes aboard the Enterprise in an officer exchange program initiated by Starfleet, much to the chagrin of the crew.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, W. Reed Moran, Drew Deighan

title: Skin Of Evil
description: A scientist's experiment in time goes awry, reuniting Picard with an old flame, now married to the scientist.
writer: Deborah Dean Davis, Hannah Louise Shearer, Tracy Tormé

title: Sleeping Dogs
description: Enterprise tries to rescue a Klingon ship from sinking into a gas giant.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Fred Dekker, Phyllis Strong

title: Soldiers of the Empire
description: Dax and Worf accompany Martok on his first command since being held by the Dominion. But the ship they are given hasn't seen victory in months and the crew is near mutinous.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, David Weddle

title: Someone to Watch Over Me
description: Capt. Janeway recalls her ancestor, Shannon O'Donnell, with great reverence, but historical records don't back up the family story.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Bryan Fuller

title: Sons and Daughters
description: Serving with his recruit son aboard a Klingon vessel, Worf finds his relationship with his son strained. Meanwhile, strained relationships abound on DS9 between Major Kira and Gul Dukat.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bradley Thompson, David Weddle, Trivia, Star Trek: The Next Generation (1987), Ira Steven Behr

title: Sons of Mogh
description: Unfair working conditions and pay cuts cause Rom to organize a union of the employees of Quark's Bar. Meanwhile, Worf still finds it hard to settle on the station.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe, Barbara J. Lee

title: Space Seed
description: The Enterprise investigates a planet whose colonists should be dead, but are not.
writer: D.C. Fontana, Jerry Sohl

title: Spectre of the Gun
description: The crew of the Enterprise pursues a mysterious woman who has abducted Spock's brain.
writer: Gene L. Coon, Arthur H. Singer

title: Spirit Folk
description: For the holographic inhabitants of Fair Haven, unworldly actions from Voyager personnel fit only one explanation - they're faerie folk.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bryan Fuller, Robert Doherty

title: Spock Amok
description: A personal visit causes a comedy of errors during Spock & Pike's crucial negotiations with an unusual alien species.

title: Spock's Brain
description: As punishment for ignoring their warning and trespassing on their planet, the Melkot condemn Capt. Kirk and his landing party to the losing side of a surreal recreation of the 1881 historic gunfight at the OK Corral.
writer: Gene L. Coon, Arthur H. Singer

title: Stardust City Rag
description: The La Sirena crew reach Freecloud and find Bruce Maddox in a precarious situation, so Seven of Nine, the ex-Borg they recently picked up, lends her assistance.

title: Starship Down
description: A damaged Defiant must play a dangerous game of cat-and-mouse with two Jem'Hadar ships inside a gas giant.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, David Mack, John J. Ordover

title: Starship Mine
description: When the Enterprise puts in to space dock for an energy sweep of the ship which is lethal to humans, Picard gets trapped on board with technicians who are not what they appear to be.
writer: Morgan Gendel, Brannon Braga, René Echevarria

title: Starstruck
description: Even with the guidance of their hologram advisor Janeway, the crew of The Protostar is tested when their ship is on a dangerous cosmic collision course.

title: State of Flux
description: When Harry disappears during his Beowulf holonovel, Chakotay and Tuvok, sent to investigate, disappear as well. Not wanting to lose anymore crewmembers, the Doctor's transferred to the holodeck to investigate.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Naren Shankar

title: Statistical Probabilities
description: A group of genetically engineered humans are brought to Dr. Bashir on DS9 in the hopes of integrating them into wider society.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, Pam Pietroforte

title: Stigma
description: T'Pol is diagnosed with a possibly fatal neurological disease as a result of a mind meld. Meanwhile, one of Phlox's wives visits Enterprise and becomes very interested in Trip.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Rick Berman, André Bormanis

title: Storm Front
description: Following the destruction of the Xindi weapon, the Enterprise crew discovers that they have been sent back in time to 1944. However, history has been altered, leaving Nazi Germany in control of a large portion of the eastern United States.
writer: Gene Roddenberry

title: Storm Front, Part II
description: After Silik attacks Trip, Vosk wants to make a deal with Archer: supplies of the ship for the completion of his time machine in return for a trip to the 22nd Century.
writer: Gene Roddenberry

title: Strange Bedfellows
description: Kai Winn gets unknowingly involved with Gul Dukat, surgically altered to resemble a Bajoran, using the name 'Anjohl.' Tensions further escalate between Weyoun and Legate Damar, as the Dominion gather to sign a peace treaty with the Breen, which includes granting the Breen certain Cardassian territories. Having been held captive by the Dominion, Ezri and Worf try to escape from the Cardassian home world.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, David Weddle

title: Strange Energies
description: The U.S.S. Cerritos is dispatched on a mission which results in an encounter with "strange energy."

title: Strange New World
description: Archer sends an away team to a habitable, luscious planet for observation. But when a storm occurs, the team begins to experience paranoid delusions.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman, Phyllis Strong

title: Strange New Worlds
description: Captain Christopher Pike comes out of self-imposed exile to rescue an officer gone missing during a secret mission.

title: Stratagem
description: Archer and the Xindi Degra are in a shuttle. With Degra suffering from memory loss, Archer tells him they escaped together from a prison and that the Insectoids took power after destroying Earth.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman, Terry Matalas

title: Sub Rosa
description: Dr. Crusher meets the man in her dead grandmother's life when she tries to put her affairs in order, whom one of the local people warn her to stay away from by not lighting a candle that has been lit for generations.
writer: Brannon Braga, Jeanna F. Gallo, René Echevarria

title: Suddenly Human
description: On a mission to an alien training mission, they discover one of its trainees to be a human.
writer: John Whelpley, Jeri Taylor, Ralph Phillips

title: Supernova, Part 1
description: Surrounded by the Federation armada, the crew attempts to stop their ship from destroying all of Starfleet.

title: Supernova, Part 2
description: As the Federation hangs in the balance, the crew must make the ultimate sacrifice to save Starfleet's future.

title: Surrender
description: Vadic forces Picard to make an impossible choice: deliver what he can never give - or watch his crew perish. Their only salvation lies in the mind of an old friend and old foe.

title: Survival Instinct
description: A forgotten past decision confronts Seven of Nine when she meets three ex-Borg former shipmates permanently mind-linked to one another.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, Robert Doherty

title: Suspicions
description: Dr. Crusher puts her career on the line to prove a scientist's theoretical new shielding technology which may have cost him his life.
writer: Joe Menosky, Naren Shankar, Brannon Braga

title: Symbiosis
description: Counselor Troi is held captive on a deserted planet by a slick, black, oily, sentient and immortal puddle of evil.
writer: Joseph Stefano, Hannah Louise Shearer, Tracy Tormé

title: Tacking Into the Wind
description: Odo is affected by the disease threatening to eliminate his race more than he lets on, while Kira has to deal with the Cardassians' dislike of her. Even though General Martok sees Chancellor Gowron's move as part of a political vendetta, he does nothing about it. Worf suspects Gowron's military strategies are meant to humiliate Martok's command abilities, without thought the consequences have for Klingons as a whole. Meanwhile, O'Brien suggests to Bashir he announce he's found a cure to the Changelings' disease to lure someone from Section 31 to DS9.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, David Weddle

title: Take Me Out to the Holosuite
description: A Vulcan captain challenges Sisko to a game of baseball.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, David Weddle

title: Tapestry
description: When Captain Picard's artificial heart fails, he is offered the rare opportunity to go back in time and set right the mistake that led to his demise.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, Brannon Braga, René Echevarria

title: Tattoo
description: Finding a familiar cultural symbol on an away mission, Chakotay connects with an experience he had as a child and tries to contact his spirit people.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Larry Brody, Kenneth Biller

title: Tears of the Prophets
description: When Sisko is picked to head up an attack on the Cardassian homeworld, the Prophets appear to him in a vision, warning him of impending doom if he leaves the station.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, David Weddle

title: Temporal Edict
description: A new work protocol eliminating "buffer time" has the Lower Decks crew running ragged as they try to keep up with their tightened schedules. Ensign Mariner and Commander Ransom's mutual lack of respect comes to a head during an away mission.

title: Terminal Provocations
description: The lovable but awkward Ensign Fletcher makes work difficult for Mariner and Boimler. Rutherford introduces Tendi to a holodeck training program that he created.

title: Terra Nova
description: Enterprise tries to find out what happened on Terra Nova, the first colonization attempt outside Earth's solar system.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Antoinette Stella, Rick Berman

title: Terra Prime
description: Paxton threatens to destroy Starfleet Headquarters if all aliens don't leave Earth. Enterprise must shut down the verteron array on Mars, but it is extremely well defended.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Judith Reeves-Stevens, Garfield Reeves-Stevens

title: Terror Firma
description: Marooned on a deadly planet, the crew must work together with their captive Gwyn to stay alive...except the planet isn't the only thing in pursuit.

title: That Which Survives
description: Kirk and Spock are taken prisoners by a former starship captain named Garth, who now resides at, and has taken over, a high security asylum for the criminally insane.
writer: Lee Erwin, Jerry Sohl, Arthur H. Singer

title: The 37's
description: The Voyager crew discovers several people who were abducted during 1937, including Amelia Earhart, cryogenically frozen on a distant planet.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Kenneth Biller

title: The Abandoned
description: Odo tries to change the nature of a rapidly maturing infant Quark finds, while Sisko tries to break his son's relationship with a 20 year old Dabo girl.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, D. Thomas Maio, Steve Warnek

title: The Adversary
description: The Defiant is off to show the Federation's presence to the new Tzenkethi government. While underway the ship malfunctions and repeated instances leads Sisko to believe a changeling's onboard, engaging in sabotage.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: The Aenar
description: Enterprise finds out the marauder was piloted telepathically by an Aenar, a subspecies from Andoria.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, André Bormanis

title: The Alternate
description: Odo and his mentor Dr. Mora Pol discover a lifeform similar to Odo on a Gamma Quadrant planet.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bill Dial, Jim Trombetta

title: The Alternative Factor
description: Kirk draws a court martial in the negligent death of a crewman.
writer: Don Mankiewicz, Steven W. Carabatsos

title: The Ambergris Element
description: While the Enterprise investigates a water-world, Captain Kirk and Commander Spock go missing. They've been abducted by the world's inhabitants and altered into water-breathers as well.

title: The Andorian Incident
description: Archer, Trip and T'Pol are captured by the Andorians at a Vulcan monastery on P'Jem. They believe that the monastery is being used to spy on Andoria.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Fred Dekker, Rick Berman

title: The Apple
description: While returning to the Enterprise aboard the shuttlecraft, Kirk, Spock, McCoy and a seriously ill Federation diplomat find themselves kidnapped by an energized cloud.
writer: Gene L. Coon

title: The Arsenal of Freedom
description: The Enterprise encounters two neighboring cultures, one suffering from a plague, the other marketing a cure, and learns that nothing is as simple as it seems.
writer: Robert Lewin, Richard Manning, Hans Beimler

title: The Ascent
description: On their way to answer a warrant, Odo and Quark crash, and must put aside their differences to survive and signal for help.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe, David Weddle

title: The Assignment
description: A Pah-wraith possesses Keiko's body and forces Chief O'Brien to complete a task, or Keiko'll be killed.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, David Weddle, Bradley Thompson

title: The Augments
description: The Augments have yet again escaped. While Dr. Soong wants to hide the embryos, Malik has an entirely different and way more cruel plan.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman

title: The Battle
description: The Enterprise encounters Q again, and he tempts Riker by endowing him with the powers of the Q.
writer: Maurice Hurley

title: The Begotten
description: The discovery of an infant Changeling gives Odo a chance to correct what he sees as the "mistakes" in his upbringing. But as Starfleet's pressure for results grows, he has to turn to his "father," Dr. Mora.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, David Weddle

title: The Best of Both Worlds
description: Responding to a distress call on one of the Federation's outer-most colonies, the Enterprise arrives...only to find a big hole in the ground where the town used to be, and discovers the Borg are behind the attack.
writer: Ronald D. Moore

title: The Best of Both Worlds, Part II
description: Having absorbed Captain Picard and his knowledge, the Borg head for Earth, leaving Riker and the Enterprise desperate for an unanticipated way to defeat them.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, Joe Menosky

title: The Big Goodbye
description: The Enterprise visits the planet where Data was created and discovers another android like him, but when he's assembled, he's not exactly like him.
writer: Robert Lewin, Maurice Hurley

title: The Bonding
description: A startling surprise awaits the crew of the Enterprise when a mission costs the life of a crew member, who left her son behind.
writer: Ronald D. Moore

title: The Bounty
description: Now on the run, Picard and the skeleton crew of the USS Titan must break into Starfleet's most top secret facility to expose a plot that could destroy the Federation. Picard must turn to an old friend for help.

title: The Breach
description: At Dr. Phlox's request, Enterprise attempts to retrieve 3 Denobulan scientists from the planet Xantoras, where the Goverment has ordered all off-worlders to evacuate within 3 days. Tucker, Reed, and Mayweather navigate a series of labyrinthine underground caves in search of the scientists. Meanwhile, Enterprise lends assistance to a damaged ship fleeing the planet. Doctor Phlox faces an ethical dilemma when one of the refugees of the ship refuses life-saving treatment. The patient, an Antaran, holds a grudge against Phlox stemming from a war between their races that ended 300 years ago and would rather die than be treated by a Denobulan. Phlox defies Archer's direct order to force treatment because it goes against the Deobulan code to yield to "the will of the patient."
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Chris Black, John Shiban

title: The Butcher's Knife Cares Not for the Lamb's Cry
The Cage
description: Capt. Pike is held prisoner and tested by aliens who have the power to project incredibly lifelike illusions.

title: The Catwalk
description: After receiving a warning about a radioactive storm approaching Enterprise, the crew is forced to take shelter in the ship's catwalk.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman, Phyllis Strong

title: The Changeling
description: Harry Mudd returns with a plot to take over the Enterprise by stranding the crew on a planet populated by androids under his command.
writer: Stephen Kandel, David Gerrold

title: The Changing Face of Evil
description: After the Breen attack Earth, mixed emotions abound on DS9. Kai Winn reveals her true feelings regarding the Prophets to her spiritual guide, a Bajoran farmer named Anjohl, who is a surgically altered Dukat, and turns her back on them, and convinced by him to embrace the Pah Wraiths. On the home world of Cardassia, Legate Damar mounts a resistance to the Dominion.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, David Weddle

title: The Chase
description: The crew of the Enterprise must race against various rival powers to uncover an archaeological secret that explains the predominance of humanoid life forms in the galaxy.
writer: Joe Menosky, Ronald D. Moore, Brannon Braga

title: The Child
description: Deanna is impregnated by an unknown alien life-form, and Dr. Katherine Pulaski joins the Enterprise as the ship's new Chief Medical Officer.
writer: Jaron Summers, Jon Povill

title: The Chute
description: Convicted of a crime they didn't commit, Harry and Tom wind up in an unsupervised, crowded prison, a hellhole where implants ramp up everyone's aggression.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Clayvon C. Harris

title: The Circle
description: Kira spends time with Vedek Bareil on Bajor just as the Circle begins receiving arms from an unknown source.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Peter Allan Fields, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: The City on the Edge of Forever
description: When a temporarily insane Dr. McCoy accidentally changes history and destroys his time, Kirk and Spock follow him to prevent the disaster, but the price to do so is high.
writer: Harlan Ellison

title: The Cloud
description: When the Voyager crew encounters a wormhole and makes contact with a ship on the other side, it raises the crew's hopes of getting back to Earth.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bill Dial, Hilary Bader

title: The Cloud Minders
description: On a planet, looking for an urgent medicinal cure, Kirk, Spock and McCoy come across a dignified recluse living privately but in splendor with his sheltered ward and a very protective robot servant.
writer: Jerome Bixby, Arthur H. Singer

title: The Collaborator
description: Vedek Bareil's targeted by fellow Kai candidate, Vedek Winn as having been a collaborator with the Cardassians during the Occupation.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Gary Holland, Ira Steven Behr

title: The Communicator
description: Upon returning from covertly observing a pre-warp culture, Lieutenant Reed realizes he lost his communicator somewhere on the planet. Archer and Reed return to recover the lost technology, only to find the natives found it first. Captured and interrogated, the crewmen find themselves doing even more damage to the culture, who have scanned the prisoners and know them to be of another race. Meanwhile, Tucker and Mayweather try to figure out how to activate the cloak on the Suliban cell ship so they can mount a discreet rescue.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, André Bormanis, Rick Berman

title: The Conscience of the King
description: At Spock's court martial, he explains himself with mysterious footage about when Capt. Pike was kidnapped by powerful illusion casting aliens.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, John D.F. Black

title: The Corbomite Maneuver
description: Captain Kirk must learn the limits to the power of a 17-year-old boy with the psionic ability to create anything and destroy anyone.
writer: D.C. Fontana, Gene Roddenberry

title: The Council
description: Enterprise has arrived at the location of the Xindi Council and with the help of Degra, Archer can make his plea in front of the five races of Xindi. Archer needs to prove that the Sphere Builders have been telling lies. While the Primates and Arboreals are already aware of the truth, convincing the other three races will not be easy. The Reptilians and Insectoids refuse to even listen to Archer. Meanwhile, the Sphere Builders haven't been sitting still, and have arranged a secret meeting with the Reptilians.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Manny Coto, André Bormanis

title: The Counter-Clock Incident
description: The Enterprise finds itself trapped in a 'negative universe' which causes the crew to begin to grow younger and younger toward infancy.

title: The Crossing
description: Enterprise is held captive by non-corporeal beings who claim to be explorers.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Rick Berman, André Bormanis

title: The Darkness and the Light
description: A mysterious assassin begins wiping out all the members of Kira's old resistance cell.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, Bryan Fuller

title: The Dauphin
description: Wesley falls for the young future leader of Daled IV, unaware just how dangerous the girl's protective and overly restrictive guardian can be.
writer: Scott Rubenstein, Leonard Mlodinow, Melinda M. Snodgrass

title: The Deadly Years
description: The Federation clashes with the Klingon Empire over mining rights to Capella IV. A sudden coup between its warrior-minded inhabitants forces Kirk's party to flee with the now dead leader's pregnant wife.
writer: D.C. Fontana

title: The Defector
description: When a Romulan officer defects to the Federation with a warning of a impending invasion, Capt. Picard struggles to decide if he is to be believed.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, Richard Danus

title: The Devil in the Dark
description: With a war with Klingons raging, Kirk and Spock attempt to resist an occupation of a planet with incomprehensibly placid natives.
writer: Gene L. Coon

title: The Die is Cast
description: Now rejoined with his former mentor, Garak is ordered to interrogate Odo about the secrets of his people, while the joined Romulan/Cardassian attack fleet moves towards the Founders' home world on a mission of destruction.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: The Disease
description: A slight respite seems to be in order but some mysterious force is affecting the very fabric of Voyager itself. To solve the mystery this crew must retrace their steps to see what went wrong.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bryan Fuller, Nick Sagan

title: The Dogs of War
description: Odo begins recovery from the deadly disease which Dr. Bashir tells him had been engineered by Section 31 in order to wipe out the Founders' entire race, A resistance mission goes bad for Kira and Garak, and they end up hiding in the basement of the house which Garak grew up in. They soon hear Legate Damar - who had been on their mission and was hiding with them - has just become a casualty of war. The Grand Nagus has chosen Quark to become his successor, upon his impending retirement on the pleasure planet, Risa.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, Ronald D. Moore

title: The Doomsday Machine
description: The USS Enterprise encounters the wrecked USS Constellation and its distraught commodore who's determined to stop the giant planet-destroying robot ship that killed his crew.
writer: Norman Spinrad

title: The Drumhead
description: A retired admiral boards the Enterprise in an effort to determine the actions aboard the ship surrounding an act of sabotage and possible treason.
writer: Jeri Taylor, Ronald D. Moore, Joe Menosky

title: The Elysian Kingdom
description: The USS Enterprise becomes stuck in a nebula that's home to an alien consciousness which traps the crew in a fairy tale.

title: The Emissary
description: The Enterprise addresses the emergency of an old Klingon ship coming out of stasis and ready to fight the Federation. A half-Human/half Klingon emissary arrives to help, who once knew Worf intimately.
writer: Richard Manning, Hans Beimler, Thomas H. Calder

title: The Empath
description: The Enterprise discovers an apparent asteroid that is on a collision course with a planet is actually an ancient populated generation ship.
writer: Hendrik Vollaerts, Arthur H. Singer

title: The Emperor's New Cloak
description: Quark and Rom have to rescue Grand Nagus Zek who's being held captive in the alternate universe.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, David Weddle

title: The End is the Beginning
description: After reflecting on the past with Raffi, Picard hires her partner, Cristobal Rios, to help him in his search for Bruce Maddox; Soji's work on the Borg cube catches the attention of the executive director.

title: The Enemy
description: La Forge gets left behind on a storm-plagued planet when the rest of his team encounters a Romulan warrior.
writer: David Kemper, Richard Danus

title: The Enemy Within
description: The crew is infected with a mysterious disease that removes people's emotional inhibitions to a dangerous degree.
writer: John D.F. Black

title: The Ensigns of Command
description: After 111 years of silence, an extremely litigious alien race asserts its agreed upon right to wipe out a Federation colony in its territory.
writer: Melinda M. Snodgrass

title: The Enterprise Incident
description: The Enterprise reaches a Federation colony where the adults have all killed themselves but the children play without care.
writer: Edward J. Lakso, Arthur H. Singer

title: The Expanse
description: An alien probe launches a devastating attack on Earth. Archer, still being hunted by the Klingons, brings the Enterprise back home to investigate.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Rick Berman, André Bormanis

title: The Eye of the Beholder
description: While investigating the disappearance of Federation members, Kirk and his landing party find themselves placed in an alien zoo.

title: The Fight
description: As a relentless bounty hunter race closes in on Voyager, a sly alien think tank offers to devise a solution in exchange for a particular member of Voyager's crew joining them.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Taylor, Rick Berman

title: The First Duty
description: Wesley Crusher's team has an accident at Starfleet Academy. Picard offers to help a Starfleet investigation as to what happened, and begins to suspect they are hiding something.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, Naren Shankar, Brannon Braga

title: The Forge
description: After a bombing destroyed the Earth embassy on Vulcan, a group known as the Syrrannites are suspected. T'Pol receives a map from her Syrrannite mother, leading into a dangerous desert, the Forge.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Garfield Reeves-Stevens, Judith Reeves-Stevens

title: The Forgotten
description: As the crew continues to repair the ship and mourn the dead, Archer works to create trust and form a partnership with members of the Xindi council.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Chris Black, David A. Goodman

title: The Forsaken
description: Lwaxana Troi comes for a political visit and instead hounds Odo for romantic attention. Meanwhile, an alien probe wreaks havoc with the station's computer, leaving Odo and Lwaxana trapped together.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Don Carlos Dunaway, Jim Trombetta

title: The Galileo Seven
description: While Captain Kirk investigates whether an actor is actually a presumed dead mass murderer, a mysterious assailant is killing the people who could identify the fugitive.
writer: Barry Trivers

title: The Game
description: Wesley Crusher returns to the Enterprise on vacation from the Academy only to discover a mysterious alien game infiltrating and controlling the crew.
writer: Brannon Braga, Susan Sackett, Fred Bronson

title: The Gamesters of Triskelion
description: The crew of the Enterprise struggles to cope with a planet of imitative people who have modeled their society on 1920s gangsters.
writer: David P. Harmon, Gene L. Coon

title: The Gift
description: As the crew works to remove impeding Borg technologies from Voyager, their Borg guest wants out while a crew member's telekinetic abilities become too big to contain.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Lisa Klink

title: The Haunting of Deck Twelve
description: Neelix engages the four ex-Borg children in a ghost story to keep them calm during a ship-wide power shutdown.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman, Kenneth Biller

title: The High Ground
description: While visiting a planet under civil war, Dr. Crusher is taken hostage by an opposing faction of the planet's ruling government.
writer: Melinda M. Snodgrass, Richard Danus, Ronald D. Moore

title: The Homecoming
description: Word that a previously thought dead Bajoran resistance legend is still alive sends Kira and Chief O'Brien to Cardassia IV to rescue him; meanwhile "The Circle", a sect of the Bajoran provisional government that is intent on wiping out alien influence, is sneaking into power.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ira Steven Behr, Jeri Taylor

title: The Host
description: Dr. Crusher falls for someone who's not at all what she expects him to be.
writer: Michel Horvat, Ronald D. Moore, Joe Menosky

title: The House of Quark
description: Quark is forced to marry a Klingon widow after he takes credit for the accidental death of her husband, the head of a powerful Klingon House.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, Tom Benko

title: The Hunted
description: Investigating a planet applying for membership into the Federation, the crew of the Enterprise finds a group of inhabitants who used to be soldiers, but now fight for their very existence.
writer: Robin Bernheim, Richard Danus, Ronald D. Moore

title: The Icarus Factor
description: Riker's delight at being offered a command of the USS Ares turns to frustration when the man sent to prepare him for his mission is his estranged father; Worf's behavior leads Wesley to delve into Klingon tradition.
writer: David Assael, Robert McCullough, Melinda M. Snodgrass

title: The Immunity Syndrome
description: Peaceful, primitive peoples get caught up in the struggle between superpowers, with Kirk unhappily trying to restore the balance of power disrupted by the Klingons.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Don Ingalls

title: The Impossible Box
description: Picard and the crew trace Soji to the Borg cube, forcing Picard to face memories of being Locutus; Narek believes he's found a way to safely exploit information from Soji.

title: The Infinite Vulcan
description: On a planet of intelligent plantlike creatures, the clone of a human scientist clones Mr. Spock for use in a galactic peace mission.

title: The Inner Light
description: Picard awakes to find himself living in a small village where he is a well-known member of the community who is suffering from a delusion of being a starship captain.
writer: Morgan Gendel, Peter Allan Fields, Brannon Braga

title: The Jem'Hadar
description: While in the Gamma Quadrant, Sisko, Quark and a strange telepathic woman are captured by the Jem'Hadar, the soldiers of the Dominion.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ira Steven Behr, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: The Jihad
description: A team comprising various experts (Kirk and Spock among them) must recover an artifact known as the Soul of the Skorr. The theft must be kept secret or a galactic holy war could break out.

title: The Killing Game
description: After Voyager is captured by the Hirogen, the ship is turned into a massive holodeck so that the aliens can hunt members of the crew who have been fitted with new identities in various scenarios based upon Earth and Federation history.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Joe Menosky

title: The Killing Game, Part II
description: Janeway seeks to retake her ship and crew from the Hirogens.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Joe Menosky

title: The Last Generation
description: In a desperate last stand, Jean-Luc Picard and generations of crews both old and new fight together to save the galaxy from the greatest threat they've ever faced.

title: The Last Outpost
description: Everyone accurately pegs a visiting propulsion scientist as a charlatan, but only Wesley Crusher recognizes his alien assistant as the real deal.
writer: Diane Duane, Michael Reaves, Johnny Dawkins

title: The Least Dangerous Game
description: On a tropical paradise planet, Mariner questions Commander Ransom on how he structures his away team. Boimler makes a bold career decision.

title: The Lights of Zetar
description: A mysterious, twinkling mass of sapient energy ravages an important archive and Scotty's new girlfriend may be linked to it.
writer: Jeremy Tarcher, Shari Lewis, Arthur H. Singer

title: The Lorelei Signal
description: Female crew members must take over the Enterprise when the men are incapacitated by a race of sirens.

title: The Loss
description: As an unknown force drags the Enterprise, Deanna Troi struggles with the loss of her empathic abilities.
writer: Hilary Bader, Alan J. Adler, Vanessa Greene

title: The Magicks of Megas-Tu
description: At the centre of the galaxy, a race puts the Enterprise crew on trial for past grievances.

title: The Magnificent Ferengi
description: At the request of the Grand Nagus, Quark puts together a mission to rescue Quark's mother, who is in the hands of the Dominion.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, David Weddle

title: The Man Trap
description: A transporter malfunction splits Captain Kirk into two halves: one meek and indecisive, the other violent and ill tempered. The remaining crew members stranded on the planet cannot be beamed up to the ship until a problem is fixed.
writer: Richard Matheson

title: The Maquis, Part I
description: The destruction of a Cardassian ship leads Sisko, Dukat, and Sisko's old friend Cal Hudson to the fact that there is an unofficial war between the Maquis and the Cardassians.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, James Crocker, Rick Berman

title: The Maquis, Part II
description: Sisko tries to resolve the Maquis crisis by preventing a war, freeing Gul Dukat and offering the defected Hudson the chance to return.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ira Steven Behr, Rick Berman

title: The Mark of Gideon
description: After the Enterprise landing party beams down to investigate a geologically interesting planet, their ship is hurled across the galaxy. Kirk and company find a deserted outpost guarded by the deadly image of a beautiful woman.
writer: John Meredyth Lucas, D.C. Fontana, Arthur H. Singer

title: The Masterpiece Society
description: Working together to prevent the annihilation of a perfectly balanced, isolated society (with no contingency for outside visitors) may, in fact, destroy it.
writer: Adam Belanoff, James Kahn, Brannon Braga

title: The Measure Of A Man
writer: Melinda M. Snodgrass, Leonard Mlodinow, Scott Rubenstein, Trivia, Gene Roddenberry

title: The Menagerie, Part I
description: The past months have left the crew exhausted and in desperate need of a break, but does this explain McCoy's encounter with a human-sized white rabbit or Kirk crossing paths with the prankster who plagued his days at Starfleet Academy?
writer: Theodore Sturgeon

title: The Menagerie, Part II
description: The Galileo, under Spock's command, crash-lands on a hostile planet. As the Enterprise races against time to find the shuttlecraft, Spock's strictly logical leadership clashes with the fear and resentment of his crew.
writer: Oliver Crawford, Shimon Wincelberg

title: The Mind's Eye
description: Geordi is brainwashed by the Romulans to start a war between the Klingons and the Federation.
writer: René Echevarria, Ken Schafer, Ronald D. Moore

title: The Most Toys
description: When transporting a dangerous material to the Enterprise, Data is kidnapped and the crew is led to believe he is dead when the shuttlecraft he was piloting explodes en route.
writer: Shari Goodhartz, Ronald D. Moore

title: The Muse
description: Sisko must face betrayal when evidence surfaces that Kasidy is smuggling for the Maquis. Meanwhile Garak makes acquaintance with Ziyal.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, Mark O'Connell

title: The Nagus
description: The Ferengi leader, Grand Nagus Zek, comes to the station. He seems very interested in Quark's bar.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ira Steven Behr, David Livingston

title: The Naked Now
description: When the leader of an alien culture takes a romantic interest in Lt. Yar, he claims her for his own, to the dismay of his own wife, who, in turn, challenges Tasha in a fight to the death.
writer: Katharyn Powers, Michael Baron, Johnny Dawkins

title: The Naked Time
description: The Enterprise picks up untrustworthy entrepreneur Harry Mudd accompanied by three beautiful women who immediately put a spell on all the male crew members.
writer: Stephen Kandel, Gene Roddenberry

title: The Next Generation
