description: After receiving a distress call from Beverly Crusher, Jean-Luc Picard enlists help from generations old and new for one last adventure: a mission that will change Starfleet and his old crew forever.

title: The Next Phase
description: A transporter malfunction, combined with a Romulan ship under stress and a damaged cloaking device, creates the illusion that Ensign Ro and Geordi are dead, but Geordi doesn't believe it to be so, and he acts accordingly.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, Brannon Braga, René Echevarria

title: The Nth Degree
description: When assigned to investigate an unknown probe, Lt. Barclay is hit by an energy surge, through a shuttlecraft's computer, and he receives an inexplicable boost of confidence and a vast increase in his knowledge.
writer: Joe Menosky, Ronald D. Moore, David Carren

title: The Offspring
description: After returning from a cybernetics conference to the Enterprise, Data creates his own "child," much to the chagrin of his captain, and without regards to the ramifications with Starfleet.
writer: René Echevarria, Ronald D. Moore

title: The Omega Directive
description: Detection of an Omega particle puts all other priorities and Starfleet directives on hold. Janeway is duty-bound to destroy that particle above all else, but Seven objects.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink, Jimmy Diggs

title: The Omega Glory
description: The Enterprise crew investigates the disappearance of a ship's crew on a planet that is a modern version of the Roman Empire.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Gene L. Coon, John Kneubuhl

title: The Outcast
description: While aiding an androgynous race who lost a couple of members in an unmapped region of space, Riker falls for one of them, which can lead to trouble if detected, since the alien race does not endorse gender specificity.
writer: Jeri Taylor, Brannon Braga, René Echevarria

title: The Outrageous Okona
description: The Enterprise rescues the captain of a broken-down freighter, only to become involved in a dispute between feuding worlds--each demanding custody of their guest. Data seeks help from Guinan in understanding humor.
writer: Burton Armus, Les Menchen, Lance Dickson

title: The Paradise Syndrome
description: Trapped on a planet whose inhabitants are descended from Northwestern Native Americans, Kirk loses his memory and is proclaimed a God while the crippled Enterprise races back to the planet before it is destroyed by an asteroid.
writer: Margaret Armen, Arthur H. Singer

title: The Passenger
description: Rao Vantika, a captured criminal, is badly burned and dies in Bashir's arms but the criminal's captor believes that he is still alive.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Morgan Gendel, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: The Pegasus
description: Riker finds out his former commander from the starship Pegasus has orders that entail more than what is revealed to Picard, who thinks the Enterprise is en route to recover the lost ship. And Riker is ordered to secrecy.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, René Echevarria, Naren Shankar

title: The Perfect Mate
description: Ferengi machinations unleash a diplomatic gift aboard the Enterprise - the rare, empathic perfect mate, ready to bond with any male around her.
writer: Reuben Leder, René Echevarria, Brannon Braga

title: The Pirates of Orion
description: Spock contracts a fatal disease, and the medicine required for his cure is stolen by Orion pirates.

title: The Practical Joker
description: The computer of the Enterprise gains partial sentience and a taste for playing practical jokes on the crew.

title: The Price
description: While the Enterprise hosts negotiations for control of a stable wormhole, Troi forms a personal relationship with an opposing negotiator, unaware of his true being.
writer: Hannah Louise Shearer, Richard Danus

title: The Q and the Grey
description: Due to the death of the Q in their last encounter with Voyager, a Civil War has broken out among the Q continuum. A new Q needs to be produced and the mischievous Q known to the USS Enterprise has chosen Janeway as his mate.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Shawn Piller

title: The Quality of Life
description: When Geordi is assigned to survey a new mining development with Dr. Farallon, who invented an experimental mining technology, she uses a new sophisticated robot called an Exocomp to repair a power grid.
writer: Naren Shankar, L.J. Scott, Brannon Braga

title: The Quickening
description: Quark believes he's going to die, and Rom convinces him to sell his remains. After an accident, Bashir must move Keiko's fetus to Kira's womb.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, Louis P. DeSantis

title: The Raven
description: PTSD strikes Seven of Nine, causing frightening visions and prompting her, under a panic attack, to seek the Borg Collective where no Borg exist.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bryan Fuller, Harry 'Doc' Kloor

title: The Reckoning
description: Spiritualists on Bajor summon Captain Sisko to the planet surface, where they show him a stone tablet with some unknown inscriptions. Once on DS9, the inscription reveals an ancient prophecy of coming disasters surrounding the wormhole, Bajor, and DS9, as those around him voice their uneasiness about Sisko being an Emissary for the planet.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, David Weddle, Bradley Thompson

title: The Return of the Archons
description: While on patrol in deep space, Captain Kirk and his crew find and revive a genetically-engineered world conqueror and his compatriots from Earth's Twentieth Century.
writer: Gene L. Coon, Carey Wilber

title: The Royale
description: Worf, Data, and Riker find themselves trapped in a re-creation of a poorly written novel.
writer: Tracy Tormé, Leonard Mlodinow, Scott Rubenstein

title: The Savage Curtain
description: Kirk, Spock, Abraham Lincoln and Vulcan legend Surak are pitted in battle against notorious villains from history for the purpose of helping a conscious rock creature's understanding of a concept he does not understand, "good vs. evil".
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Arthur Heinemann, Arthur H. Singer

title: The Schizoid Man
description: A dying anti-social scientist unintentionally comes upon the perfect vessel in which to preserve his intellect and arrogant personality - Data.
writer: Tracy Tormé, Richard Manning, Hans Beimler

title: The Search, Part I
description: Instead of waiting for the Jem'Hadar to attack, Sisko wants to try a different tactic; he wants to take a new class starship to try to find the Founders.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: The Search, Part II
description: Odo has found his home and is introduced. Meanwhile Sisko finds out peace talks between the Dominion and the Federation have already started.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: The Serene Squall
description: While on a dangerous humanitarian mission, the crew of the Enterprise stumbles into a harrowing game of leverage with the quadrant's deadliest space pirate.

title: The Seventh
description: T'Pol asks Captain Archer to join her on a mission to capture a Vulcan fugitive that escaped her years ago.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Rick Berman, André Bormanis

title: The Ship
description: The crew is forced to retreat in a crashed Jem'Hadar ship after an attack by the Jem'Hadar. They get the feeling there's something very important about this ship.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, Pam Wigginton

title: The Shipment
description: Enterprise follows a lead and discovers a Xindi lab that refines Kemocite, the key ingredient in the weapon being built to destroy Earth. An away team of Archer, Reed and Hayes confront the lead researcher, Gralik Durr, who was employed by Degra to refine the Kemocite but was never told what it would be used for. When Gralik realizes it was his research that contributed to the 7 million deaths on Earth, he agrees to help Archer prevent further loss of life. Meanwhile, on Enterprise, Phlox and Tucker study the weapon they seized from the Xindi and find an organic component, a life-form serving as the rifle's power cell. Despite T'Pol's objection, Tucker tests the weapon.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Chris Black, Brent Friedman

title: The Siege
description: As a Circle-led Bajoran military attempts to occupy the station, a skeleton crew, led by Sisko fight to reveal the Circle's secret before they're forced to evacuate themselves. Meanwhile, Kira and Dax lead a mission to reveal the truth about the Circle, on Bajor.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: The Siege of AR-558
description: Capt. Sisko and his away team volunteer to stay with a besieged unit at an isolated outpost.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, David Weddle

title: The Slaver Weapon
description: Spock, Uhura and Sulu discover an ancient multi-use weapon and are captured by the Kzinti who are equally interested in it.

title: The Sound of Her Voice
description: As the Defiant races to a shipwrecked officer who is days away, the crew keeps in contact with her. Meanwhile, Jake follows Quark's latest scheme for story research.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, Pam Pietroforte

title: The Spy Humongous
description: Anomaly consolidation day on the U.S.S. Cerritos leaves the Lower Deckers with mixed emotions. Captain Freeman attempts to negotiate peace on the Pakled home world.

title: The Squire of Gothos
description: For bringing hostility into their solar system, a superior alien race brings Captain Kirk into mortal combat against the reptilian captain of an alien ship he was pursuing.
writer: Gene L. Coon, Fredric Brown

title: The Star Gazer
description: Starfleet must once again call on legendary Jean-Luc Picard after members of his former crew - Cristóbal Rios, Seven of Nine, Raffi Musiker, and Dr. Agnes Jurati - discover an anomaly in space that threatens the galaxy.

title: The Stars At Night
description: The Cerritos crew must prove their worth in a mission race.

title: The Storyteller
description: While Sisko tries to negotiate an agreement between two Bajoran factions, he orders O'Brien to escort Bashir to investigate an emergency that endangers a village.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kurt Michael Bensmiller, Ira Steven Behr

title: The Survivor
description: A famous humanitarian is found alive near the Romulan Neutral Zone after being missing for five years, but he may not be who he claims to be.

title: The Survivors
description: The Enterprise responds to a distress call from Rana IV, a Federation colony that is under attack from an unknown alien ship. When they arrive, they find the entire surface of the planet destroyed, save for a single house and two occupants.

title: The Swarm
description: Voyager is attacked by swarms of tiny spaceships which attach themselves to Voyager's hull. The Doctor is losing his memory and Torres activates the Jupiter Station diagnostic program run by a familiar hologram.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman, Lisa Klink

title: The Sword of Kahless
description: When a transporter emergency turns the command crew into holosuite characters, Bashir's James Bond fantasy takes on a deadly reality.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, Bob Gillan

title: The Terratin Incident
description: Exposed to spiroid epsilon waves, the Enterprise crew begins to shrink in size. If a way can not be found to stop their condition, they will soon be too small to control the ship.

title: The Thaw
description: Voyager finds a group of people in connected stasis chambers where something has gone terribly wrong.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Richard Gadas

title: The Tholian Web
description: With Capt. Kirk and the derelict USS Defiant apparently lost, the Enterprise grapples with an insanity causing plague and an attack by the Tholians.
writer: Judy Burns, Chet Richards, Arthur H. Singer

title: The Time Trap
description: The Enterprise and a Klingon ship are trapped in space-time warp where only their cooperation will allow them to escape.

title: The Trouble with Tribbles
description: Capt. Kirk obsessively hunts for a mysterious cloud creature he encountered in his youth.
writer: Art Wallace

title: The Ultimate Computer
description: Kirk and a sub-skeleton crew are ordered to test out an advanced artificially intelligent control system - the M-5 Multitronic system, which could potentially render them all redundant.
writer: D.C. Fontana, Laurence N. Wolfe

title: The Vengeance Factor
description: The Enterprise pursues a race of "gatherers" who continue to raid Federation outposts, unaware of the dangers lurking among them.
writer: Sam Rolfe, Richard Danus

title: The Visitor
description: Bashir is asked to help a group of renegade Jem'Hadar break their addiction to ketracel white. Meanwhile Worf is dissatisfied with the way Odo runs security.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink, Nicholas Corea

title: The Void
description: Almost the entire Voyager crew has been abducted and brainwashed to serve as workers in a severely short-handed industrial alien society.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bryan Fuller, Michael Taylor

title: The Voyager Conspiracy
description: Modifying her alcove to process several months of gathered data at a time turns Seven into a rampant conspiracy theorist. Meanwhile, Janeway deals with an alien scientist developing catapult technology.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Robert Doherty

title: The War Without, The War Within
The Way of the Warrior
description: Sisko becomes uncomfortable when the Klingons station a task force to help defend against the Dominion. Worf is summoned to find out their true intentions.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: The Way to Eden
description: A group of idealistic hippies, led by an irrational leader, come aboard the U.S.S. Enterprise.
writer: Arthur Heinemann, D.C. Fontana, Arthur H. Singer

title: The Wire
description: Dr. Bashir fights to save Garak's life when a device implanted in his brain, designed to alleviate pain in the event of torture, begins to malfunction and is slowly killing him.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: The Wounded
description: After being fired upon by a Cardassian vessel, Picard races against time to find out the facts behind the Cardassian commander's claim that the Federation attacked one of their outposts.
writer: Jeri Taylor, Stuart Charno, Sara B Cooper

title: The Xindi
description: Enterprise has been in the Delphic Expanse for six weeks and have yet to get any intel about the Xindi. Strange anomalies affect the ship, sending cargo flying violently through the bays, pockets of atmosphere losing gravity and other incidents defying the known laws of physics. T'Pol helps Trip's insomnia with an intimate Vulcan therapy. Archer and Trip find another race of Xindi imprisoned in a mine.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Rick Berman, André Bormanis

title: These Are the Voyages...
description: In 2370, Commander William T. Riker is trying to clear his mind and relives the last mission of the first Enterprise on the holodeck.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Rick Berman, Brannon Braga

title: Thine Own Self
description: Data is sent to follow a probe that has drifted off course and crashed on a primitive planet, only to crash on the planet himself, with no memory of any event prior to crashing on the planet's surface.
writer: Ronald D. Moore, Christopher Hatton, René Echevarria

title: Things Past
description: Sisko, Dax, Odo and Garak find themselves in the past when the Cardassians controlled the station, and a crime that Odo investigated may be the key.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Taylor, David Weddle

title: Think Tank
description: Voyager must prevent a toxic waste freighter from exploding, lethally contaminating a vast sector of space. Unfortunately, there could be an alien boogeyman aboard the heavily irradiated ship.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bryan Fuller, Nick Sagan

title: Thirty Days
description: In a letter to his father, Tom Paris tells the story of the events leading up to his demotion to Ensign and sentence to thirty days in Voyager's brig.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Scott Smith Miller

title: This Side of Paradise
description: The Enterprise is sent to a mining colony that is being terrorized by a mysterious monster, only to find that the situation is not that simple.
writer: Gene L. Coon

title: Threshold
description: Tom's attempt to cross the time warp threshold and make a name for himself results in rapid physical mutation.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Michael De Luca

title: Through the Looking Glass
description: Sisko is kidnapped and forced to impersonate his deceased mirror universe counterpart in order to convince Jennifer Sisko to defect to the Terran Rebellion.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Ties of Blood and Water
description: When a Cardassian official, who thinks of Kira as his daughter, is dying, he agrees to reveal all he knows to her. But the experience dredges up Kira's memories of her own father's death.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Hewitt Wolfe, Edmund Newton

title: Time Amok
description: When an anomaly fractures the U.S.S. Protostar in time, Hologram Janeway must synchronize the disjointed crew and save their ship before it destructs.

title: Time Squared
description: The Enterprise comes across its own shuttlecraft adrift in space - a duplicate - with an unconscious duplicate Capt. Picard inside.
writer: Kurt Michael Bensmiller, Melinda M. Snodgrass

title: Time and Again
description: Searching to replenish their dilithium supplies, Voyager encounters the Vidiians who assault other races for their organs. Neelix is attacked and his lungs taken. Now it's a race against time to retrieve the stolen lungs and save his life.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Skye Dent, Brannon Braga

title: Time's Arrow
description: Captain Picard and his archaeological curiosity are called upon by scientists from Earth when they find evidence to support beliefs that aliens had visited Earth in the late 1800s.
writer: Joe Menosky, Brannon Braga, René Echevarria

title: Time's Arrow, Part II
description: Captain Picard and crew follow Data back to the late 1800s to get him back, only to find the suspected alien visitors killing people of that time.
writer: Jeri Taylor, Joe Menosky, Brannon Braga

title: Time's Orphan
description: The Chief's daughter gets caught in a time displacement and when they get her back, she is considerably older.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, David Weddle, Bradley Thompson

title: Timeless
description: A miscalculation by Ensign Kim causes a fatal crash during Voyager's first test with slipstream travel. Fifteen years in the future, survivors Chakotay, Kim and The Doctor attempt to send a message back in time to prevent the tragedy.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Rick Berman

title: Timescape
description: Aboard a shuttlecraft and en route back to the Enterprise, Picard, LaForge, Data, and Troi find themselves trapped in a time trap of some sort, where the Enterprise is being taken over by Romulans, and the ship, itself, on a course of destruction.
writer: Brannon Braga, René Echevarria, Naren Shankar

title: Tin Man
description: The Enterprise reaches out to an alien being, while dancing around with the Romulans, who want to attack it in an act of vengeance.
writer: Dennis Bailey, David Bischoff, Ronald D. Moore

title: Tinker Tenor Doctor Spy
description: An alien race, sizing up Voyager for a raid, taps into The Doctor's cognitive subroutines to make him their spy, unaware they're watching The Doctor's new daydreaming program.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Bill Vallely

title: To the Death
description: Bashir tries to help a planet in the grips of a Dominion-engineered plague that guarantees a painful death.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Naren Shankar

title: Tomorrow is Yesterday
description: Seeking the answer to a century-old mystery, Kirk and crew encounter a vacantly peaceful society under a 6000-year autocratic rule that kills all those it can't absorb.
writer: Boris Sobelman, Gene Roddenberry

title: Too Short A Season
description: A planet that was able to cloak itself for thousands of years suddenly reveals itself, with its inhabitants proposing peace. But, after initial negotiations, children of the Enterprise are kidnapped due to the infertility of the inhabitants.
writer: Hannah Louise Shearer

title: Transfigurations
description: The Enterprise finds a deserted planet where a ship has crashed, and, with it, the lone survivor with no memory, but extraordinary healing powers.
writer: René Echevarria, Ronald D. Moore

title: Treachery, Faith and the Great River
description: A Vorta offers Odo valuable information on the Dominion in exchange for asylum. Meanwhile, Nog tries his Ferengi knowledge as he wheels and deals for a valuable piece of equipment for Chief O'Brien.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, David Weddle, Bradley Thompson

title: Trials and Tribble-ations
description: Sisko tells two men from Temporal Investigations how he and his crew went back in time to when Captain James Kirk of the first Starship Enterprise exposed a Klingon spy with the help of Tribbles.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, René Echevarria

title: Tribunal
description: Chief O'Brien goes through the horrific Cardassian judicial system when he is charged for an unknown crime.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Bill Dial, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: True Q
description: When an honor student in the medical field is assigned to the Enterprise in an internship manner, she begins to demonstrate powers of the Q, prompting you-know-who to show up.
writer: René Echevarria, Matthew Corey, Brannon Braga

title: Trusted Sources
description: A visiting reporter on the Cerritos puts Captain Freeman on edge.

title: Tsunkatse
description: Seven of Nine and Tuvok are enslaved while on an away mission, with Seven forced to fight in gladiatorial competitions to the death.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Doherty, Gannon Kenney

title: Turnabout Intruder
description: Captain Kirk's insane ex-lover Dr. Janice Lester forcibly switches bodies with him in order to take command of the Enterprise.
writer: Arthur H. Singer, Gene Roddenberry

title: Tuvix
description: Transporter trouble merges Tuvok and Neelix into one, creating Tuvix.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Andrew Price

title: Twilight
description: His memory crippled, Archer awakens years in the future, after the Xindi have reduced Earth to a cinder and the remnants of humanity have settled on a distant planet. Now, Archer learns that he may be the key to undoing this tragedy.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Sussman, André Bormanis, Connections, Treksperts Briefing Room: Star Trek: Enterprise Writers Room Reunion (Live at STLV)

title: Twisted
description: A strange spatial distortion afflicts the ship and has the entire crew literally lost on their own ship.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Arnold Rudnick

title: Two Days and Two Nights
description: The members of the Enterprise crew are faced with various misadventures and challenges while taking a two day break from their duties on the planet of Risa.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Chris Black, Rick Berman

title: Two of One
description: Tallinn helps Picard and the crew infiltrate a gala on the eve of a joint space mission to protect an astronaut they believe is integral in restoring the timeline - Renee Picard; Kore makes a startling discovery about her father's work.

title: Unexpected
description: After helping to repair the warp drive on board of a Xyrillian ship, Trip notices a strange bulging on his arm.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Rick Berman, Phyllis Strong

title: Unforgettable
description: An alien woman from a closed world seeks asylum aboard Voyager, claiming she's been there before and that she and Chakotay were lovers, but no one remembers her.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink, Greg Elliot

title: Unification I
description: Picard and Data follow Spock into Romulan space on a dangerous mission.
writer: Jeri Taylor, Rick Berman, Brannon Braga

title: Unification II
description: On Romulus, Picard finds Spock, as well as an underground peace movement, a double agent, a bold ulterior motive against the Federation, and a familiar adversary.
writer: Rick Berman, Brannon Braga, René Echevarria

title: Unimatrix Zero
description: Simultaneously, as Voyager nears an alien outpost decimated by the Borg, Seven of Nine begins to dream vividly of an idyllic sanctuary where a few Borg can gather subconsciously - Unimatrix Zero.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Michael Sussman

title: Unimatrix Zero, Part II
description: Although captured, Capt. Janeway squares off with the Borg Queen after successfully introducing the Doctor's pathogen to the Borg Central Plexus. Supply the cure or watch thousands die.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Joe Menosky

title: United
description: Captain Archer must convince Vulcans, Tellarites and Andorians to unite to find the Romulan ship.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Judith Reeves-Stevens, Garfield Reeves-Stevens

title: Unity
description: Chakotay finds a planet of unassimilated Borg drones from all over the galaxy.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Lisa Klink

title: Unnatural Selection
description: Enhanced DNA developments trigger an epidemic of rapid aging. Caught among its victims - Dr. Pulaski.
writer: John Mason, Mike Gray, Leonard Mlodinow

title: Up The Long Ladder
description: An antiquated distress signal leads to two lost 22nd century Earth colonies, each facing doom in different ways, one by fire, the other by prolonged cloning.
writer: Melinda M. Snodgrass

title: Valiant
description: Jake and Nog's runabout come under attack from the Jem'Hadar, and are rescued by the Valiant, a ship manned by Red Squad, an elite group of young Starfleet cadets.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, David Weddle

title: Vanishing Point
description: After Hoshi uses the transporter to avoid running into a storm, she starts to feel very strange.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Rick Berman, André Bormanis

title: Veritas
description: Mariner, Boimler, Tendi, and Rutherford are caught off guard when aliens force them to testify about a series of seemingly unrelated events.

title: Violations
description: Transporting three Ullian mind-probing historians, the Enterprise crew is stymied when some of its members fall into a coma. Deanna Troi, the first crew member to come through it, cannot recall anything surrounding the incident.
writer: Pamela Gray, Jeri Taylor, Shari Goodhartz

title: Virtuoso
description: The Doctor's singing talent is so appreciated by an alien species that he considers resigning his commission and staying on with his millions of adoring fans.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Raf Green, Kenneth Biller

title: Vis à Vis
description: Tom Paris befriends an amiable alien, unaware he's a serial form-exchanger who switches appearances and troubles with others without permission.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Robert Doherty, Lisa Klink

title: Visionary
description: While a Romulan delegation tries to get information about the Dominion, O'Brien sees of unpleasant things that are about to happen in the future.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, John Shirley, Ethan H. Calk

title: Vortex
description: A man named Croden tells Odo he can take him to a place where aliens much like Odo himself exist, which would help Odo find out where he truly comes from.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Sam Rolfe, Robert Hewitt Wolfe

title: Vox Sola
description: A fibrous, web-like alien creature comes aboard the ship and several of the crew becomes entangled in it including Archer and Trip, and it seems Hoshi is the only one who can try to communicate with the creature.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Fred Dekker, Rick Berman

title: Võx
description: A devastating revelation about Jack alters the course of Picard's life - and uncovers a truth that threatens every soul in the Federation. Picard and his crew race to save the galaxy from annihilation - at a gut-wrenching cost.

title: Waking Moments
description: Nightmares herald the presence of an alien race who've mastered the dream state. Chakotay's experience in lucid dreaming seems Voyager's best hope of escaping a shared dream entrapment.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, André Bormanis, Lisa Klink

title: Waltz
description: After being attacked by Cardassian forces, Sisko gets stranded with a very psychotic Gul Dukat, who was being transported to his trial. Meanwhile the crew of the Defiant races to rescue survivors.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, David Weddle

title: Warhead
description: Voyager discovers another Federation starship in the Delta Quadrant, one that's had a rougher time getting home, on its last legs, and harboring a dark secret.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Joe Menosky, Rick Berman

title: Warlord
description: A dying warlord, determined to retake his home planet, takes over Kes's body.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Lisa Klink, Andrew Price

title: Watcher
description: With time running out to save the future, Picard seeks out an old friend for help; Rios winds up on the wrong side of the law; Jurati makes a deal with the Borg Queen.

title: We'll Always Have Paris
description: After diverting to a secret meeting with an old friend and some of Starfleet's finest commanders, Picard finds the Horatio blown to bits just hours after the meeting and he returns the Enterprise to Earth looking for answers.
writer: Tracy Tormé, Robert Sabaroff, Hans Beimler

title: We'll Always Have Tom Paris
description: Assigned a special task by Dr. T'Ana, Tendi enlists Mariner for help. On the U.S.S. Cerritos, Rutherford is consumed by a bridge crew mystery.

title: Wej Duj
description: As the U.S.S. Cerritos crew has downtime during a long warp trip, a Klingon ship and a Vulcan one find themselves unknowingly heading to an encounter with the Starfleet ship.

title: What Are Little Girls Made Of?
description: Kirk and psychiatrist Helen Noel are trapped on a maximum security penal colony that experiments with mind control and Spock must use the Vulcan mind-meld to find a way to save them.
writer: Shimon Wincelberg

title: What You Leave Behind
description: As the Dominion War comes to an end, Dukat goes to the Pah Wraiths to awaken them. Meanwhile the Dominion turn on the rebelling Cardassians, destroying them city by city. Will the Alliance prevail over the Dominion? Will Garak and the Rebellion stop them before the destruction of Cardassia? Will Sisko stop the Pah Wraiths from rising?
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, David Weddle

title: When It Rains...
description: Kira gets sent behind Cardassian lines in an effort to train them in resistance fighting. When Dr. Bashir learns of the deadly disease Odo is infected with, he meets with resistance whilst trying to obtain Odo's medical records from Starfleet. Martok gets blindsided by Gowron when he arrives on DS9, announcing he has assumed command of the Klingon fleet.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, René Echevarria, Spike Steingasser

title: When The Bough Breaks
description: On Velara III, Geordi and Data discover a microscopic life form responsible for the death of an engineer stationed on the base.
writer: Robert Sabaroff, Karl Geurs, Ralph Sanchez

title: Where No Man Has Gone Before
description: Dr. McCoy discovers his old flame is not what she seems after crew members begin dying from a sudden lack of salt in their bodies.
writer: George Clayton Johnson

title: Where No One Has Gone Before
description: While distracted by two antithetical races applying for Federation membership, the Enterprise picks up a sentient entity that can alternately possess either man or machinery.
writer: D.C. Fontana, Michael Halperin

title: Where Pleasant Fountains Lie
description: Mariner & Boimler are stranded on an uninhabited planet with a sentient computer. On the Cerritos, Lt. Commander Billups must prove his engineering abilities to an old adversary.

title: Where Silence Has Lease
description: The Enterprise encounters a mysterious void in space and when they move in closer to investigate further, it envelops them and they can't get out.
writer: Jack B. Sowards

title: Whispers
description: Chief O'Brien's world is turned upside down when for no reason whatsoever he is being ignored by his family and friends and is being closed out of every essential job on the station.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Paul Robert Coyle, Robert Hewitt Wolfe, Trivia

title: Who Mourns for Adonais?
description: A transporter accident places Captain Kirk's landing party in an alternate universe, where the Enterprise is in the service of a barbarically brutal empire.
writer: Jerome Bixby

title: Who Mourns for Morn?
description: A former patron of Quark's bequeaths everything to him.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Mark O'Connell, David Weddle

title: Who Watches The Watchers
description: A proto-Vulcan culture worships Captain Picard and prepares to offer Counselor Troi as a sacrifice.
writer: Richard Manning, Hans Beimler

title: Whom Gods Destroy
description: Kirk beams down to the planet Gideon and appears to find himself trapped on a deserted Enterprise. Spock on the real Enterprise must use his diplomatic skills to deal with the uncooperative inhabitants of Gideon and find the Captain.
writer: George F. Slavin, Stanley Adams, Arthur H. Singer

title: Wink of an Eye
description: While transporting an arrogant, demanding princess for a political marriage, Captain Kirk must cope both with her biochemical ability to force him to love her, as well as sabotage on his ship.
writer: John Meredyth Lucas, Arthur H. Singer

title: Wolf in the Fold
description: Very alien visitors to our galaxy attempt to connect with human consciousness but miss, winding up tapping into the regions of human nightmares instead.
writer: Robert Bloch, D.C. Fontana

title: Workforce
description: While B'Elanna's memories slowly return aboard Voyager, Chakotay struggles to maintain his identity against the Quarren's Division 6.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Michael Taylor, Bryan Fuller

title: Workforce, Part II
description: Using the holodeck, Seven of Nine is trying to perfect her social skills to the point of becoming an obsession.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, André Bormanis

title: Worst Case Scenario
description: B'Elanna's discovery of a holonovel projecting a Maquis takeover of Voyager enthralls the crew, but a hidden edit could kill its mystery author.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Kenneth Biller, Lisa Klink

title: Wrongs Darker Than Death or Night
description: After receiving some disturbing information from Gul Dukat, Major Kira seeks Sisko's approval as Emissary to travel back in time to find out the truth about her mother.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Hans Beimler, David Weddle

title: Year of Hell
description: Voyager comes across a Krenim timeship that's wiping whole species from existence to change the existing timeline.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Joe Menosky

title: Year of Hell, Part II
description: A year after Voyager encounters the Krenim time ship, a badly damaged Voyager with a skeleton crew leads an armada of interplanetary ships against them.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Brannon Braga, Joe Menosky

title: Yesterday's Enterprise
description: The Enterprise C enters the Enterprise D's time and space continuum, where they find Picard and crew in a constant state of war with the Klingons, and only Guinan knows it.
writer: Ira Steven Behr, Richard Manning, Hans Beimler

title: Yesteryear
description: After finding himself erased from recent history, Spock must travel back in time to save himself as a youth.

title: You Are Cordially Invited
description: Suddenly desiring to wed within the week on DS9, Worf and Jadzia go through their own Klingon rituals: Worf on a four day bachelor party with the male members of the crew & Jadzia deals with her prospective mother-in-law, who totally opposes the idea of "an alien" marrying into her Klingon household.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Ronald D. Moore, David Weddle

title: Zero Hour
description: Archer takes Degra's ship to try to prevent Earth from being destroyed and gets some unexpected help. Enterprise tries to destroy Sphere 41, but the Sphere Builders know about the plan.
writer: Gene Roddenberry, Rick Berman, André Bormanis
